text:
This housing application opportunity adds to the others foreseen for a.a. 2026-27:
- [Dedicated to Freshmen - Olympic Village](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/dedicated-freshmen "Dedicated to Freshmen")

- [Preferential rate new admission application - open to all Bocconi students](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/preferential-rate-ay-2026-27 "Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27")
Application available from 17-24 February 2026.

Available only for students who are already guests at full rate as of 17 February 2026

[Online application](https://admissions.unibocconi.it/admissions/Students/OpenFromExtUrl?concourseId=14338&ordType=)

Application sessions available starting from March-April 2026

[Find out more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/full-rate-housing-new-admission-application-ay-2026-27)

Application available starting from September 2026

information shortly available

[Find out more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en)

1\. Requirements

A full rate assignment may be requested only by students who:
- **are non-resident**, meaning living in a municipality from which it is possible to reach the location of the study course attended by public transportation, with travel times exceeding 90 minutes(\*)
The only exception are students interested in applying during the Open reservation, for whom this requirement does not have to be strictly fulfilled.
- **Not fall under any of the** [**grounds for exclusion**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/grounds-exclusion-full-rate-housing-ay-2026-27 "Grounds for exclusion - full rate housing a.y. 2026-27")

- **having earned the minimum number of credits** as per paragraph 2 "Credits required to submit the application"

- **enrolled for the a.y. 2026-27:**

metadata.title: Full rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Full rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
>in the first, second, or third year of a Bachelor of Science program

>in the first, second, third, fourth, or fifth year of the Integrated Master of Arts in Law

>in the first, or second year of a Master of Science program.

**Students who are recipient of financial aid that includes** free **accommodation**(e.g., Una Scelta Possibile) or accommodation at reduced rate (e.g., Moving to Milan), upon acceptance of the benefit, renounce the possibility of receiving any another type of housing assignment (preferential rate, full rate) for the academic year of the first assignment and subsequent years (if the financial aid is renewed). Acceptance will result in the automatic cancellation of any housing request submitted and/or of any housing assignment received. It is also specified that after acceptance, no further housing requests of any type can be submitted.

The number of rooms available at full rate is usually lower than the applications submitted. Regularly enrolled students will be considered as a priority: applications submitted by students transferring from other Universities or repeating any year of course will not be accepted.

(\*) The travel times to be calculated are the minimum times required, based on the public transport route, between the departure station/stop from the place of residence and the arrival station/stop at the study location, including waiting times at intermediate stops. Walking routes or private transportation are excluded. These times can be verified on the regional website [www.muoversi.regione.lombardia.it](http://www.muoversi.regione.lombardia.it/) and, for cities outside the Lombardy Region, through the Google Maps search engine at [www.google.it/maps](http://www.google.it/maps)

2\. Credits required to submit the application

Exclusively for applications for full rate housing renewal applications, a minimum number of credits —detailed below— will be verified, which the student must have in order to apply. However, please note that **no** minimum number of credits is required for:

>First year students in a.y. 2026-27 of the Bachelor or Integrated Master of Arts in Law programs are not required to reach this minimum number of credits if they wish to apply for the "Full rate new admission application (for 1st year students")

>Students who wish to apply for the "Full rate new admission application (for all year students) application.

>Students who wish to apply for the Open reservation application.

In the " **Full rate housing renewal**" application session, only applications from students who have obtained the number of credits specified below by the following deadlines will be considered: **February 3, 2026** for bachelor and master of science, and **March 25, 2026** for integrated master of arts in law programs

**Minimum number of credits required**

**11 credits** for those enrolled in the first year (bachelor, master of science and integrated master of arts in law programs) in a.y. 2025-26

**60 credits** for those enrolled in the second year (bachelor and integrated master of arts in law programs) in a.y. 2025-26

**120 credits** for those enrolled in the third year of a bachelor programs in a.y. 2025-26and intending to enroll in the first year of a master of science program in Bocconi University for a.y. 2026-27, or for those enrolled in the third year of an integrated master of arts in law program in a.y. 2025-26

**180 credits** for those enrolled in the fourth year of an integrated master of arts in law program in a.y. 2025-26

metadata.title: Full rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Full rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
It is specified that for the achievement of the minimum number of credits required at the end of the winter session there is NO use of bonus credits in addition to the credits actually earned by the student.

For students who have participated in an Exchange program during the first semester, the timing of recognition of exams taken abroad will be considered. Such students are invited to still apply for housing renewal if interested and to monitor the credit recognition in order to complete it as soon as possible. Please note that no flexibility can be applied in relation to the achievement of the merit requirement for accommodation - credits to be achieved by August 10, 2026.

It is also specified that for students with disability pursuant to Italian Law n.104/1992 or with civil disability equal or greater than 66%, the number of minimum credits may deviate up to a maximum of 40% from the number of credits indicated above.

3\. Application submission

The housing assignment for a.y. 2026-27 is granted for the academic year of application only, therefore, it is not automatically renewed for subsequent academic years, but it is the student's responsibility to check whether it can be renewed and to make subsequent applications for assignment in the ways that will be provided and detailed on the website for each academic year. Throughout the period in which the student is a resident in a Bocconi residence hall, room changes are not permitted. Instead, it is the right of Bocconi University to transfer the student, even during the year, to another residence hall at the rate provided for the new residence.

Before accessing the online application:

>please see the useful documents available at this [**link**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/useful-documents)

>keep your login credentials on hand (username and password)

>have a fast Internet connection and use a single device

**Full rate renewal application a.y. 2026-27**

**from 17-24 February 2026**

Available only for students who are already guests at full rate as of 17 February 2026.

**ONLINE APPLICATION**

**Full rate new admission application a.y. 2026-27**

Starting from March/April 2026

Rooms available in the Bocconi residence halls are limited. First year students (of Bachelor programs or of the Integrated Master of Arts in Law program) have a priority: in fact, the first application session is reserved to them. All other assignment sessions can be accessed by all students. In case of non-reservation due to the exhaustion of rooms in a specific session, it will still be possible to reapply in the following sessions. Please see the dedicated webpage with the Calendar of application sessions available for a.y. 2026-27 and carefully read the Instructions to submit the application.

**Monthly Open reservations a.y. 2026-27**

starting from September 2026

Please see the dedicated webpage with the **calendar of application sessions** available for a.y. 2026-27 and carefully read the Instructions to submit the application.

Applications submitted after the deadline and/or with submission methods that do not comply with the prescribed procedure and/or with incomplete documentation will not be taken into consideration.

After submitting your application, monitor in the "Notifications" area of MyApplication for communications from the Office, which may indicate the need for additions or integrations of documents.

4\. Results and acceptance

**Result housing renewal application**

The result will be made available within early April 2026 on MyApplication. For the renewal application, no subsequent steps of acceptance are required, the room is assigned.

**Result housing new admission application and acceptance**

The information in the following paragraph is valid for all the new admission application sessions (1st year students and subsequent years students) and for the monthly open reservations.

metadata.title: Full rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Full rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
The result of a housing new admission application is published on MyApplication usually around 3 working days after the session.

If the result is positive, you will receive a full rate housing assignment for a.y. 2026-27. On the result, you will be notified the residence assigned, the room type, the period of assignment and the fee applied. The accommodation offer received is not changeable and no room changes are foreseen.

If you wish to accept the offer of accommodation received, proceed to pay the security deposit of 600 euros and upload all the required documents by the date that will be indicated in the result. If you fail to complete all the steps required to accept the housing assignment received by that deadline, your assignment will automatically be cancelled.

5\. Requirements needed to use the accommodation

Students who will result housing assignees for a.y. 2026-27 following the procedure described above will be able to use the assigned accommodation only in presence of the following conditions:

**1) REGULAR ENROLLMENT for a.y. 2026-27**

\> regular enrollment for the absolute first time for a.y. 2026-27 in the first regular year of a Master of Science (i.e. bachelor Bocconi students going to the Master of Science program)

or

\> regular enrollment for a.y. 2026-27 for the absolute first time in a regular year subsequent to the first of a Bachelor of Science, Master of Science, Integrated Master of arts in Law or PhD program.

It is specified that the years of enrollment are counted for each study cycle starting from the absolute first year of enrollment\*.

It is specified that for students with a disability pursuant to Italian Law n.104/1992 or with civil disability equal or greater than 66% can reside in the Bocconi Residence Halls at a preferential rate also if enrolled for a.y. 2025-26 in the 2nd year “fuori corso” of a Bachelor of Science, Master of Science, Integrated Master of Arts in Law program.

\*The absolute first year of enrollment shall be understood as any academic year of first enrollment in any kind of degree program (even if different from the one attended in the a.y. 2026-27) which required the same education qualification as the one needed to access the attended program in the a.y. 2026-27, in any University in Italy or abroad, even if the program has not been completed.

**2) MERIT REQUIREMENT: CREDITS TO BE OBTAINED AND REGISTERED BY AUGUST 10, 2026**

It is specified that for the "New admission" and the "Monthly Open reservation" application sessions, the fulfillment of this requirements is not required.

For the purpose of eligibility to use the accommodation, the rules established in terms of merit requirements and detailed on the [**dedicated webpage**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/merit-requirements-ay-2026-27 "Merit requirements a.y. 2026-27") for the different types of housing applications apply.

6\. Payment

Before your arrival at the residence for a.y. 2026-27 you will have to pay the **housing 1st installment**: the deadline will be August 5th, 2026. You will see the amount due on your you@B once you'll have completed your enrollment for a.y. 2026-27. We recommend you to make the payment through the **Flywire tool** system.

metadata.title: Full rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Full rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
[**Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2026-27**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/fees-deadlines-and-methods-payment-ay-2026-27 "Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2026-27").

Please consider that the calculation of the housing installment is carried out at the beginning of July for all housing assignees, regardless of their situation in terms of reaching the merit and the enrollment requirement (see point 5) that the office cannot verify before August 10. The possibility of seeing the due payment on you@B does **not** entail an unconditional room assignment, which is always subject to the possession of the enrollment and merit requirements. In absence of the requirements needed to use the assigned room, the office will proceed with the cancellation of your housing assignment, but you will still be required to pay the first installment (it will not be possible to cancel the due amount).

In the event you will pay the amount due but after a subsequent verification the merit and enrollment requirement will not be met, the amount you paid will not be refunded and the housing assignment will be cancelled in any case.

Please remember that the University grants the right to leave the accommodation early and to withdraw from the commitment to such payment, exclusively in the foreseen cases of renunciation of the accommodation, proven by the specific academic reasons required for the acceptance of the renunciation request, and withdrawal from the accommodation, according to what detailed on the dedicated website pages.

7\. Cancellation in case of lack of the requirements

Students assigned housing for whom it is subsequently determined that they did not regularly enroll for the a.y. 2026-27 or did not meet the merit requirements will lose their right (eligibility) to use the Housing Service for the a.y. 2026-27. The assignment will be cancelled by the office and students will be required to leave the assigned accommodation immediately. It is specified that the payments due will remain the responsibility of the student even in the event of cancellation of the assignment. For this reason, before submitting a housing application, students are invited to pay particular attention to the provisions regarding the [**cancellation**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/cancellation-and-revocation-ay-2026-27 "Cancellation and Revocation a.y. 2026-27") of the housing assignment, specified on the dedicated webpage.

8\. Residence halls calendar and extraordinary openings

The Bocconi residence halls will be open from the end of August 2026 to the end of June 2027. The standard assignment period for the 2026-27 academic year will meet the academic needs of most students. To stay during the special opening periods (winter break 2026 and July 2027), you will need to submit a separate request (information available soon).

There are no time restrictions on entry or exit for residence hall guests. There are restrictions, however, for external guests, whose presence in the residence hall is allowed only between 7:00 a.m. and midnight.

**Important note regarding check-out**

Regardless of the end date of your housing assignment, we remind you that **at the end of your housing assignment period, you must completely clear your room and apartment of all your belonging** s, even if you will return to the same room the following year. In fact, as specified in the Bocconi Residences Regulations:

metadata.title: Full rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Full rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
_At the time of check-out for the end of the assignment period or during the year, the student is required to completely empty their room and other areas of their accommodation (bathroom, kitchen, living room, where present), including completely emptying refrigerators and freezers. Bocconi University is not responsible for goods left in the apartment/room after check-out._

Attention: rooms are reassigned to Summer School students during the summer, so it is not possible to leave anything inside your room or apartment. **Any issues related to missing or deteriorated items during the summer, a period when the room is not assigned, cannot be considered by Bocconi University or the residence management.**

9\. Students with disabilities

Bocconi University has accommodations in each residence hall that are designed to accommodate students with disabilities in order to offer them the possibility to easily live their university experience in a collective context that meets their needs. Students with disabilities who require special housing needs may contact the Office of Fees, Funding and Housing, which will evaluate, with each student, which housing is best suited to their specific needs. All reported issues and provided documentation will be treated confidentially and subject to applicable privacy regulations. In order for the Office to be able to satisfy the requests received, it is best to clarify your needs in advance. Interested students are advised to contact the Fees, Funding and Housing Office before submitting their housing application.

text:
Applying to Bocconi

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi)

Bachelor and Law Programs

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs)

Staying in one of the 7 university residences provided by the University is the best way to fully experience your university life. You will easily meet other students and share your daily life with students from all over the world, in a multicultural, lively, and stimulating environment.

The residences offer **well-maintained spaces, practical services, and an international community**. They are all located in the **city center**, within a maximum walking distance of 10–15 minutes from the classrooms where lectures take place. All our residences offer single bedrooms, with either a private bathroom or one shared with at most one other student, and access to a shared kitchen.

On this webpage, all the details about requirements, methods and deadlines of application, results and requirements needed to use the accommodation are explained. Therefore, **before applying**, be sure to carefully read the contents of **paragraphs 1-9** on this page.

You can access the application you are interested in through the boxes at the top of the page; links will be available during the application period.

Places available in the Bocconi residences are limited. If no reservation is made, it is possible to apply for one or more subsequent sessions. In preparation for application, all interested students are advised to:

\> Keep your login credentials (matriculation/username and password) on hand, have a fast Internet connection, and use a single device;

\> Carefully read the entire paragraph entitled "Instructions for submitting the application".

Instructions for submitting the application

Carefully read the instructions for filling out the online application before submitting it.

> ACCESS the link at the indicated opening time: you will be in a virtual "waiting room". When your turn comes, log in by entering your Bocconi credentials (student number/Username and password).

> ENTER in the "Accommodation choice" section.

> SELECT THE RESIDENCE

If you do not see any options, it means that there are no longer available places.

\> SELECT ROOM TYPE

If you do not see any options, it means that there are no longer available places.

> CLICK ON "SAVE" at the bottom of the section.

If you can't click it means that there are no longer available places.

> CLICK ON "CONFIRM DATA / SUBMIT DATA"

If you are unable to click it means that there are no longer available places.

> The reservation was successful only if the red "Confirm data/Submit data" button is no longer visible and in its place appears the words, "You confirmed the data on dd/mm/yyyy."

> UPLOAD DOCUMENTS AND SUBMIT APPLICATION

Proceed by 11:59 p.m. on the same day to upload all documents available in application to be printed, hand signed and uploaded in the upload spaces provided, and to pay the security deposit. Click on "Submit application/ Submit application" by 11:59 p.m.

The office will verify that your reservation was successful and it will give it a result through MyApplication in the following days.

**ATTENTION**

If you were not able to click on "Save" at the bottom of the section, it means that there are no longer available places. Proceed to select a new room type and/or Residence.

If you have succeeded in selecting a Residence and room type and saved the section, but at the time of "Submit data" another student has taken the last available place for the Residence and/or type you selected, the red "Submit data" button remains visible: this means that, even if you have already expressed and saved a preference in the "Residence and room type" section, your reservation was not successful due to lack of availability.

Because of this, you were not able to confirm the data, so you will have to:

-re-enter the "Residence and Room Type" section;

-select a new option;

-save the data on the section again by clicking on "SAVE";

-confirm the data for the new selection by clicking on the red "Submit data" button.

If in searching for a new option to select all available places are taken, the system would continue to show you, in the "Residence and Room Type" section, your original selection: this does NOT mean that the reservation was successful.

Instructions for submitting the application

Carefully read the instructions for filling out the online application before submitting it.

**Step 1 - to secure the room**

> ACCESS the link at the indicated opening time: you will be in a virtual "waiting room". When your turn comes, log in by entering your Bocconi credentials (student number/Username and password).

> ENTER in the "Accommodation choice" section.

> SELECT THE RESIDENCE

If you do not see any options, it means that there are no longer available places.

\> SELECT ROOM TYPE

If you do not see any options, it means that there are no longer available places.

> CLICK ON "SAVE" at the bottom of the section.

If you can't click it means that there are no longer available places.

> CLICK ON "CONFIRM DATA / CONFERMA DATI"

If you are unable to click it means that there are no longer available places.

The reservation was successful only if the red "Confirm data/Conferma dati" button is no longer visible and in its place appears the words, "You confirmed the data on dd/mm/yyyy."

**Step 2 - to confirm the reservation**

steps to be completed by 11.59 pm on the same day

> COMPLETE THE SECTIONS in which data are required

\> UPLOAD DOCUMENTS

Proceed by 11:59 p.m. on the same day to upload all documents available in application to be printed, hand signed and uploaded in the upload spaces provided, and to pay the security deposit.

> CLICK ON “INOLTRA DOMANDA / SUBMIT APPLICATION”

Click “INOLTRA DOMANDA/ SUBMIT APPLICATION” by 11:59 PM on the same day.

Your reservation is successfully confirmed only if the red “Inoltra domanda / Submit application” button is no longer visible and is replaced by the message: “You submitted the application on dd/mm/yyyy.”

The office will verify that your reservation was successful and it will give it a result through MyApplication in the following days.

**ATTENTION**

If you were not able to click on "Save" at the bottom of the section, it means that there are no longer available places. Proceed to select a new room type and/or Residence.

If you have succeeded in selecting a Residence and room type and saved the section, but at the time of "Submit data" another student has taken the last available place for the Residence and/or type you selected, the red "Submit data" button remains visible: this means that, even if you have already expressed and saved a preference in the "Accommodation choice" section, your reservation was not successful due to lack of availability.

Because of this, you were not able to confirm the data, so you will have to:

-re-enter the "Accommodation choice" section;

-select a new option;

-save the data on the section again by clicking on "SAVE";

-confirm the data for the new selection by clicking on the red "Submit data" button.

If in searching for a new option to select all available places are taken, the system would continue to show you, in the "Accommodation choice" section, your original selection: this does NOT mean that the reservation was successful.

Applying to Bocconi

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi)

Bachelor and Law Programs

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs)

Bocconi Residence Halls

Staying in one of the 7 university residences provided by the University is the best way to fully experience your university life. You will easily meet other students and share your daily life with students from all over the world, in a multicultural, lively, and stimulating environment.

The residences offer **well-maintained spaces, practical services, and an international community**. They are all located in the **city center**, within a maximum walking distance of 10–15 minutes from the classrooms where lectures take place. All our residences offer single bedrooms, with either a private bathroom or one shared with at most one other student, and access to a shared kitchen.

The room assignment period will be **valid from 20 August 2026 to 27 June 2027**.

Places available in the Bocconi residences are numerous but limited. If no reservation is made, it is possible to apply for one or more subsequent sessions..

**Once a room has been reserved in any previous assignment session, including those for the Olympic Village, it is not possible to reserve another one.**

In preparation for application, all interested students are advised to:

> Check the different types of residences and rooms and their rates a.y. 2026-27

> Take a look at the [useful documents](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/useful-documents "Useful documents") (regulations and privacy policy);

\> Keep your login credentials (matriculation/username and password) on hand, have a fast Internet connection, and use a single device;

\> Carefully read the entire paragraph entitled "Instructions for submitting the application".

**Application online**

Below you can find the list of application sessions available for a.y. 2026-27. The link to access the online application will be published on the day each session opens, and will only be active from the opening time indicated.

It is specified that only students who have NOT already confirmed a room reservation in any previous assignment session, including those for the Olympic Village, may participate in this application session.

If you are interested in the Olympic Village, you can take part in the applications outlined and indicated on the [dedicated page](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/dedicated-freshmen "Dedicated to Freshmen").

text:
\*Non-resident students are students residents in a municipality from which it is possible to reach the location of the study course attended by public transportation, with travel times exceeding 90 minutes. The travel times to be calculated are the minimum times required, based on the public transport route, between the departure station/stop from the place of residence and the arrival station/stop at the study location, including waiting times at intermediate stops. Walking routes or private transportation are excluded. These times can be verified on the regional website [www.muoversi.regione.lombardia.it](http://www.muoversi.regione.lombardia.it/) and, for cities outside the Lombardy Region, through the Google Maps search engine at [www.google.it/maps](http://www.google.it/maps)

Applying to Bocconi

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi)

Bachelor and Law Programs

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs)


A security deposit of € 600.00 is required from students who accept the housing assignment to guarantee the fulfillment of all commitments made with the acceptance itself.

Payment of the security deposit must be made by credit card through StoreUB, following the instructions available within the housing application at the time of the reservation.

Please note that students who renew  the accommodation occupied will not have to repeat the payment of the previously paid security deposit.

The security deposit will be returned to the student, without interest, and thereafter at the time the student finally vacates their assigned place at the end of the academic year, but no later than January of the following year. This refund timeline will apply to all housing assignments related to the standard assignment period (end of August — end of June), including annual open reservations.

The deposit will be refunded under condition that the student has completed all the due housing payments and that he/she has no debts towards Bocconi University or the residence management. In addition, in case the student will not take in charge the possible restoration costs for damages made to the structures of the residence or for recovery of sanitary and general conditions of the room, the housing deposit will also be used for covering the related expenses.

When foreseen, the housing deposit refund will be made on a bank account in the student’s name that he/she will need to register on Punto Blu (Home > Personal data > Data Bank Account > Refunds).

The security deposit will NOT be refunded and will be kept as a penalty deposit in case of withdrawal from the housing assignment.

The security deposit will NOT be refunded in cases of housing renunciation not substantiated by the specific academic reasons provided for the acceptance of the request for withdrawal. More details on this page of the website. The security deposit will NOT, moreover, be returned in cases of revocation and cancellation of housing assignment.

[VIALE ISONZO 23](https://www.google.it/maps/place/Residenza+Isonzo/@45.4464294,9.198049,17z/data=%213m1%214b1%214m5%213m4%211s0x4786c417c271c295:0x2638fba69944e8cf%218m2%213d45.4464257%214d9.2002377)

The Isonzo Residence is located a 15-minute walk from campus and close to the M3 subway line. It was inaugurated in September 2014 and it has two units: Isonzo 23 and Torre Isonzo.

The **Isonzo Residence** has:

**A.** 11 single rooms with a private bathroom;

**B.** 86 single rooms with a gender-specific bathroom shared with another student;

The **Torre Isonzo Residence** has:

**C.** 116 rooms in 4-bedroom apartments with 2 bathrooms and a shared living room/kitchen area.

**Description of the spaces**

All rooms are furnished with a bed, a desk, a wardrobe and are equipped with WiFi internet. Blankets, sheets, pillowcases and towels are provided. Linens are changed and rooms are cleaned once a week. Guests in Isonzo Residence may use shared kitchens located on each floor or kitchen located in the common areas of the apartments. In addition, there are study rooms, break rooms, a coin laundry area and a relax area.

## Dorm amenities


### Wifi


### Laundry


### Study area


### Recreation area

Why live in University housing?

First of all, **location**: university residence halls are on or near campus, close to classes and the university facilities, and just minutes from the center of town.

Another important advantage of dorm life is constant **interaction** with your fellow students, Italian and international alike. You'll meet lots of different people with whom you'll have many things in common - interests, ambitions and dreams - in addition to your studies. It will be friendly environment where you'll soon feel at home.

Only students who are attending Bocconi University or participating in exchange programs are allowed to access the residence halls.

Plus, living in one of the university residence halls is both **safe and convenient**. All our residence halls have a doorman ready to help you 24/7, and the services included in your lodging fee will allow you to maintain your busy schedule without worrying about the electric bill or changing the sheets.

All Bocconi residence halls are closed during summer period, according to the period of suspension of courses and exams.

What the residents love about the Isonzo Residence

- The Isonzo Residence is located close to areas of great interest and development;
- It is located close to the Metro Station.

How to book a room?

**Booking a room in a Bocconi residence is simple**: participate in one of the online applications planned for the 2026-27 academic year, at preferential or full rate. Please check the information available on our homepage at the following [link](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing "Housing").

[Join Us at Our Open Day](https://bocconiopenday.liveforum.space/membership/login?lang=en-GB&source=float_login "Join Us at Our Open Day")

text:
Applying to Bocconi

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi)

Bachelor and Law Programs

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs)

Accommodation availability and Summer School opportunity

_The content of this page refers exclusively to the options available to students already residing in the 7 Bocconi residences during the 2026–27 academic year._

Standard accommodation assignments end for everyone on **27 of June, 2027**.

It will not be possible to request an extension in your current assignment for the month of July, but any student who has already been assigned accommodation in one of the Bocconi residences in June 2027 may still request a room in the residence indicated on this page, subject to availability. Please read the paragraphs below carefully with regard to check-out in June and the options available for July.

Check out June 2027

Regardless of the end date of your housing assignment, we remind you that **at the end of your housing assignment period, you must completely clear your room and apartment of all your belonging** s, even if you will return to the same room the following year. In fact, as specified in the Bocconi Residences Regulations:

_At the time of check-out for the end of the assignment period or during the year, the student is required to completely empty their room and other areas of their accommodation (bathroom, kitchen, living room, where present), including completely emptying refrigerators and freezers. Bocconi University is not responsible for goods left in the apartment/room after check-out._

Attention: rooms are reassigned to Summer School students during the summer, so it is not possible to leave anything inside your room or apartment. **Any issues related to missing or deteriorated items during the summer, a period when the room is not assigned, cannot be considered by Bocconi University or the residence management.**

In case of need of storage, we suggest you to evaluate very common luggage storage services in Milan, such as BoxOK, Red Box Storage, Radical storage, Casaforte Milano

Staying in Milan: book your accommodation

Students already staying in the residence halls during the a.y. 2026-27 (during the month of June of 2027) who need to stay in Milan in July as well, the University has reserved an entire building which will be able to **accommodate all students in need of staying in Milan in July**. In August, however, all Bocconi residence halls will be closed.

It will be possible to check the availability and **book a room for the month of July** through a dedicated application, available around February until all available places are filled. All those who book will be required to pay the July monthly rate based on the same rate applied (full or preferential) depending on their annual assignment a.y. 2026-27.

Rooms will be assigned following the application order and until all available places are filled. In the event that the number of requests exceeds the availabilities, the office will try to find an alternative solution.

More information soon available

Collaborative Opportunities: Summer School Residence Ambassadors

As part of the Bocconi Summer School, starting in the summer of 2027, the University is offering students who are already staying in the residences to actively participate in the project by applying to serve as “Summer School Residence Ambassador.” Selected students will be able to stay in their original residence until the end of July 2027 free of charge and will receive a cash compensation to cover small expenses.

More information soon available

[Join Us at Our Open Day](https://bocconiopenday.liveforum.space/membership/login?lang=en-GB&source=float_login "Join Us at Our Open Day")

metadata.heading_path: July 2027 - Bocconi University

text:
The **bathroom** will be as follows, depending on the type of room:
- single room with private bathroom: private for exclusive use
- studio apartment: private for exclusive use
- single room with shared bathroom: shared with only one student of the same gender
- single room in a 2-bedroom apartment: shared with only one student of the same gender
- single room in a 4-bedroom apartment: shared with only one student of the same gender
As for the **kitchen**, here is how it is provided for each type of room:
- single room with private bathroom: has access to a shared kitchen located on the same floor, which serves approximately 15-20 students
- single room with shared bathroom: has access to a shared kitchen located on the same floor, which serves approximately 15-20 students
- Studio apartment: has a private kitchenette located inside the apartment.
- Single room in a 2-bedroom apartment: has a private kitchen, which is shared only with another bedroom. In this case, the room is shared with a student of the same gender.
- Single room in a 4-bedroom apartment: has a private kitchen, which is shared only with 3 other bedrooms. Please note that in this case, sharing may be between 4 students of the same gender, but also between students of the opposite gender: it is possible that 4-room apartments may be assigned to 2 males and 2 females.
When living in residence, you will always have access to a kitchen where you can prepare your meals: it may be inside your apartment or shared on your floor, depending on the type of accommodation you have booked.

All kitchens in Bocconi residences are equipped with cooking stoves, so you can only use pots and pans marked with the relevant symbol. Plates, glasses, and other kitchenware are not provided: you can decide to bring them from home before you arrive, although we suggest you wait until you arrive at the residence so that you can organize sharing the purchase or use of these items with your roommates. Near the residences, you will find supermarkets and other well-stocked stores where you can buy everything you need, either for yourself or to share.

To ensure a positive experience for everyone, it is essential that all guests cooperate in **leaving the kitchen tidy and clean after each use**: tidying and cleaning must be carried out by each guest after preparing their meal. The cleaning service included in the fee certainly covers the kitchens, but the staff will not be able to complete it properly if the kitchens are found cluttered with dirty dishes and pots.

All residences have common areas where you can spend your free time, focus on your studies, or relax with your friends. In particular, you will find:
- study room: dedicated to individual or group study and reserved for residence guests,
- games room: a recreation area designed in collaboration with student representatives for socializing with other residence guests,
- relaxation room: where you can do bodyweight exercises or relax after a long day of studying.

text:
All Bocconi Residences and campus buildings are equipped with drinking water dispensers. Check [this page](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/about-us/sustainability/drink-sustainably "Drink Sustainably!") to see where they are located and commit to reducing plastic consumption. In addition, in the break areas of the residences, you will find vending machines selling sweet and savory snacks, coffee machines, and even vending machines selling personal care products.

All residences have a laundry area with automatic washing machines and dryers. Each wash and dry cycle is subject to a fee, based on the rates displayed in the laundry rooms of each residence. Ironing boards and irons are not provided.

All Bocconi Residences offer a 24-hour reception desk service. After checking in and settling into your accommodation, you can also receive mail at the reception.

The common areas of the apartments, bathrooms, and bedrooms are cleaned once a week. The cleaning service for the common areas of the Residence is daily. As a rule, rooms and apartments are cleaned by floor: upon arrival, check which day your accommodation will be cleaned. In particular, the following services are provided: cleaning of bathrooms, cleaning of the kitchen and living area, change of linen (sheets and towels), and bed making. You will be provided with: a blanket, sheets, and face towels. If you prefer, you can bring your own linens, which you will need to wash/change yourself. Please note that staff will only clean the accommodation if it is tidy.

If you require technical maintenance in your accommodation, you must submit a request via the dedicated portal by scanning the QR codes located in each room. Submitting the request will allow the residence management to intervene promptly to resolve the problem you have encountered, replacing any defective parts or carrying out all necessary checks with the relevant technicians. Please note that the residence management and Bocconi University staff will be able to access your accommodation even in your absence in case of necessity or urgency.

All utilities (electricity, heating/air conditioning, water) are included in the accommodation fee, as is WiFi connection, which is guaranteed in all common areas and rooms. Please refer to Article 16 of the Residence Regulations and the Addendum for some important notes on the use of the connection.

Bocconi University is committed to ensuring the health and safety of all its students, both on campus and in its residences, in order to prevent accidents and minimize all risks associated with university life.

In order to live in the residences, it is essential to be aware of the potential risks that may arise from the improper use of the facilities and equipment provided in the building or owned by guests. The cooperation of all guests is essential to prevent dangers arising from improper behavior (inappropriate use of kitchens, use of electrical appliances or tools that are not provided and are potentially dangerous, smoking outside of designated areas).

When it comes to safety in the residence, there are many topics to address, such as:
- Fire risk
- Evacuation rules
- Accidents in the apartment
- What to do in case of accident/illness
- What to do in case of flooding
Check all the safety rules and regulations in the residence and if you have any doubts, contact the manager.

Bocconi has set up a medical center on campus, a medical clinic on the first floor of the building at Piazza Sraffa 11. The medical center will primarily serve the needs of non-residents of Milan and those who have difficulty accessing their own primary care physician, with the following important notes:

metadata.title: Life in the Bocconi dorms a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Life in the Bocconi dorms a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
- The medical center is NOT an emergency service; in case of emergency, always call the European emergency number 112 or the Bocconi emergency number +39 0258362121, which will activate the ambulance service if necessary.
- This is a first-level internal medicine service that can provide medical examinations with reports, medication, and prescriptions (however, it CANNOT issue prescriptions covered by the National Health Service, as by law this can only be done by your family doctor).
- This service is only available to members of the Bocconi community and cannot be extended to others (family members and acquaintances).
Schedules, procedures, and contact information are available in the yoU@B Agenda in the Health and Safety widget and on the Intranet. For further information, please contact [hse@unibocconi.it](mailto:hse@unibocconi.it).

**Live your residence in GREEN mode**

We care about respecting the environment: less waste, more attention to consumption, and proper waste management. It's up to you to do your part too!

♻️ **Separate waste collection**

In your room, apartment, and common areas, remember to separate your waste:
- Organic → brown
- Paper/cardboard → white
- Glass → green
- Plastic/metal → yellow
- Non-recyclable → black
If you have any doubts, take a look at the **AMSA** website: it will only take you 2 minutes.

👉 NB: PCs, smartphones, and small appliances do NOT go in unsorted waste: take them to collection points (recycling centers or CAM).

🚫 **Less plastic and paper**
- Use the water fountain in the Residence instead of plastic bottles.
- Only print if really necessary.
- Choose recycled materials.
⚡ **Be mindful of consumption**
- Turn off lights when not in use.
- Do not leave windows open with the heating/AC on.
- Do not waste water.
🏠 **Respect the spaces (they are your home!)**
- Dispose of waste in the correct bins.
- Clean and tidy up after using the common areas.
- Do not throw food in the sink.
- Keep your room tidy.
- Do not damage furniture or rooms: other students will be here after you!
❗ **Important**

If the accommodation is found to be in **poor condition**, the management may:
- Charge you for **extra cleaning**;
- Give you an **official warning**;
- In serious cases → **expulsion** from the Residence, loss of deposit, and referral to the Disciplinary Committee.

metadata.title: Life in the Bocconi dorms a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Life in the Bocconi dorms a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
The temperature control system in university residences is set in accordance with municipal ordinances governing the correct temperatures to be maintained in buildings, as well as the maximum number of hours allowed for the operation of the systems, with the aim of reducing energy consumption and limiting environmental impact.

The system operates according to a technical protocol implemented in 2017, which regulates the activation of air conditioning systems using standardized parameters, in particular:

for heating, a set point of 19 degrees Celsius is set, which is the temperature indicated as compliant by the Municipality of Milan, with a margin of tolerance of 1-2 degrees.

The activation of air conditioning depends on the outside temperature, which must reach at least 26 degrees Celsius and ONLY if the window is closed (when the window is open, the system switches off automatically).

The system operates during specific time slots, which are shared with guests by the manager of each residence and updated seasonally.

Any malfunction of the systems must be reported promptly using the dedicated QR code: in this way, technicians will immediately check for any faults or the need to replace spare parts in the individual appliances in the rooms (fan coils), or they will be able to carry out all the necessary checks in the event of centralized problems.

The perception of heat/cold is entirely personal, but the settings applied are certainly suitable to ensure everyone's thermal comfort. Bocconi is confident that it can count on the sensitivity of all members of its community to protect environmental sustainability.

Bocconi University has accommodation in each residence designed to accommodate students with disabilities, offering them the opportunity to enjoy their university experience in a communal setting that meets their needs. Students with disabilities who have special accommodation requirements can contact the Fees, Funding and Housing Office, which will work with each student to assess the most suitable accommodation for their specific needs. All issues reported and documentation provided will be treated confidentially and subject to current privacy regulations. In order for the Office to be able to meet the requests received, it is advisable to specify the needs in advance. Interested students are therefore advised to contact the Fees, Funding and Housing Office before submitting their accommodation application.

text:
Applying to Bocconi

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi)

Bachelor and Law Programs

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs)

Bocconi Residence Halls

_The content of this page refers exclusively to the 7 Bocconi residences. The partner facility Olympic Village provides services detailed on the dedicated page (Dedicated to Freshmen) and on the operator’s pages._

Bocconi University is committed to welcoming you into an environment designed to make Milan your second home: the university residences offer well-maintained spaces, practical services, and an international community that allows you to fully enjoy the university experience. Living in a residence means having the opportunity to share moments of study and leisure with students from all over the world, building relationships that enrich your personal and academic journey. It is a stimulating experience, but one that requires a willingness to engage and share: **living together means respecting others, accepting compromises, and adapting your habits for the good of the group.** In this way, you will develop valuable transferable skills (flexibility, mediation skills, team spirit) that will be useful not only during your years of study but also in your future career.

Before making this choice, **consider whether this lifestyle aligns with your needs and well-being**: it is the foundation for successfully navigating the university journey that awaits you.

Find out here what to expect from our facilities and all the useful information for your stay in residence.

When your accommodation is assigned, you will be informed of the type of room you have been assigned.

The bedrooms at Bocconi-owned residences are all **single** rooms and are furnished with a bed, bedside table, desk, and wardrobe.

text:
- **3rd year Bocconi students a.y. 2024-25**
For students enrolled in the 3rd year or 1st year fuori corso of a Bocconi Bachelor of Science program in a.y. 2024-25 that will enroll in the first year of a Bocconi Master of Science program in a.y 2025-26, housing renewal is not subject to merit requirements.

**3. Merit requirement: specific rules for calculating credits**
- **Bonus**
In order to reach the minimum merit requirements needed, in addition to the credits already earned, all students, except first-year students, may use a bonus, as indicated below:
- If students have never used a bonus previously, they can request bonus credits as indicated in the table above. Once students use bonus credits they no longer have the right to accrue other bonus credits.
- Once students use bonus credits, they still have the possibility to use any residual credits in future years.
- Students enrolled in Master of Science programs can use any residual bonus credits not used during their Bachelor of Science program. If they never used bonus credits during their Bachelor of Science program, they may use up to 15 credits.
- **Calculation of credits**
The number of necessary credits will be calculated based on the credits required for each past academic year, starting with the absolute first-year of enrollment\[1\]. This rule also applies to anyone who has interrupted the normal progression of the attended years of the program (e.g. students who repeat or have repeated the enrollment in a program year already attended, also after having withdrawn from studies or having transferred from other Universities or faculties, or for other interruptions), except for “temporary interruptions” pursuant to art. 8 section 5 of the D.P.C.M. 9 april 2001.
- **Course change**
For study program changes, for calculating merit, will be considered only credits valid for the Bachelor of Science, Master of Science or Integrated Master program attended when the student submits the housing application
- **Students with disability**
Merit criteria for students with disability pursuant to Italian Law n.104/1992 or with civil disability equal or greater than 66%

may deviate up to a maximum of 40 percent

from the set. Exceptionally, such students will be able to

renew housing even if for a.y. 2025-26 they will enroll in the first year fuori corso (full rate renewal) and second year fuori corso (preferential rate only) of their program.
- **PhD program**

metadata.title: Merit requirements a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Merit requirements a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University

text:
PhD students who meet the economic requirements can apply for housing renewal or new admission for a.y. 2025-26 if they have been regularly admitted for a.y. 2025-26.

**Attention:**

Please note that the lack of the merit requirement will result in the [**cancellation**](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/housing/2024-25+cancellation+housing) of the housing assignment. Students who, at any time during the year, are found to lack the merit requirement will be asked to leave their accommodation immediately and will still be required to pay in full any housing installments still due, or will not be entitled to a refund of housing installments if already paid. They will also not be entitled to a refund of the security deposit.

Please check the possibility of submitting a **housing renunciation application** if you realize that you might not reach the credits needed for the merit requirement by August 10, 2025. Please make sure to check all the details on the dedicated **renunciation** webpage.

\[1\] The first absolute year of enrollment shall be understood as any academic year of first enrollment in any kind of degree program (even if different from the one attended in a.y. 2025-2026) which required the same education qualification as the one needed to access the attended program in a.y. 2025-2026, in any University in Italy or abroad, even if the program has not been completed.

**3. Merit requirement: specific rules for calculating credits**

In order to reach the minimum merit requirements needed, in addition to the credits already earned, all students, except first-year students, may use a bonus, as indicated below:
- If students have never used a bonus previously, they can request bonus credits as indicated in the table above. Once students use bonus credits they no longer have the right to accrue other bonus credits.
- Once students use bonus credits, they still have the possibility to use any residual credits in future years.
- Students enrolled in Master of Science programs can use any residual bonus credits not used during their Bachelor of Science program. If they never used bonus credits during their Bachelor of Science program, they may use up to 15 credits.
- **Calculation of credits**

This rule also applies to anyone who has interrupted the normal progression of the attended years of the program (e.g. students who repeat or have repeated the enrollment in a program year already attended, also after having withdrawn from studies or having transferred from other Universities or faculties, or for other interruptions), except for “temporary interruptions” pursuant to art. 8 section 5 of the D.P.C.M. 9 april 2001.
- **Course change**
For study program changes, for calculating merit, will be considered only credits valid for the Bachelor of Science, Master of Science or Integrated Master program attended when the student submits the housing application
- **Students with disability**
Merit criteria for students with disability pursuant to Italian Law n.104/1992 or with civil disability equal or greater than 66%

may deviate up to a maximum of 40 percent

from the set. Exceptionally, such students will be able to

renew housing even if for a.y. 2026-27 they will enroll in the first year fuori corso (full rate renewal) and second year fuori corso (preferential rate only) of their program.
- **PhD program**

text:
In order to satisfy all the need of students of the Right to University, according to the housing application timeline for a.y. 2025-26, a new "recovery" session of Open reservation has been introduced. This session will allow students who will result eligible for the ISU Bocconi Scholarship a.y. 2025-26 during the autumn 2025 to request the housing rate change with respect to the rate already applied for a.y. 2025-26 (if already assignees of a room at full rate) or to request a housing new admission at preferential rate for a.y. 2025-26 (if not yet assignees of a room).
|     |
| --- |
| Only students who will result eligible for the ISU Bocconi Scholarship a.y. 2025-26 will be entitled to participate to the Open reservation — recovery session (preferential rate) |
By participating to the Open reservation — recovery session, one of the following requests may be submitted:

**1\. Housing rate change for a.y. 2025-26**

This request may be submitted only by students who:
- are assignees of a room at full rate for a.y. 2025-26 (in any previous assignment session);
- resulted eligible for the ISU Bocconi Scholarship a.y. 2025-26.
In case the request will be accepted:
- the preferential rate will be applied starting from the second housing installment and there will be no adjustment of the first housing installment already paid;
- the rate change will be applied only if the student who is requesting it will be eligible for the ISU Bocconi Scholarship a.y. 2025-26;
- the rate change will be applied only if the room occupied by the student for a.y. 2025-26 is one of the room types available also at preferential rate.
It is specified that the rate change will be subject to availability of rooms that can be assigned at preferential rate possibly left after all the previous preferential rate applications, or after the "Renunciation/ Withdrawal" period, and always up to the number of rooms defined in the Housing Application Requirements and Regulations a.y. 2025-26.

**2\. Preferential rate new admission housing application for a.y. 2025-26**

This request may be submitted only by students who:
- are NOT assignees of a room at full rate for a.y. 2025-26
- resulted eligible for the ISU Bocconi Scholarship a.y. 2025-26
The housing assignment, valid from January 2026, will be subject to availability of rooms that can be assigned at preferential rate and up to the number of rooms defined in the Housing Application Requirements and Regulations a.y. 2025-26. For these room assignments, all the preferential rate rooms possibly left after all the previous preferential rate applications (included the housing rate change requests) or after the "Renunciation/ Withdrawal" period, will be used.

In the event that the number of applicants will exceed the number of rooms available, the room assignments will happen by giving priority to:
- non resident students
- 1st year students
- increasing amount of the ISEE/ISEEU parameter

metadata.title: Open reservation - recovery session a.y 2025-26 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Open reservation - recovery session a.y 2025-26 | Bocconi University

text:
**HOW TO SUBMIT THE APPLICATION**

This application may be submitted in the period **from 5 to 9 November, 2025** through the dedicated link that will be published on this page of our website.

[**Online application Open Reservation Recovery Session**](https://admissions.unibocconi.it/admissions/Students/OpenFromExtUrl?concourseId=14139&ordType=)

Before accessing the application, **please see the useful documents** (Provisional Housing Application requirements and regulations a.y. 2025-26, Bocconi residence regulations a.y. 2025-26, Privacy policy) available at the following [link](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/current-students/services/useful-documents-0).

Results

The results of the Open reservation - recovery session application for the 2025-26 a.y. will be communicated on MyApplication.

In the event of a **request of housing rate change** with a negative result due to non-availability of assignable housing at preferential rate (as specified above), the student may apply for Early withdrawal by and no later than **November  2025**.

In the event of a **preferential rate new admission housing application** with a negative result due to non-availability of assignable housing at preferential rate, the student may apply for the Open reservation at full rate (during the first session in **December 2025**).

[Join Us at Our Open Day](https://bocconiopenday.liveforum.space/membership/login?lang=en-GB&source=float_login "Join Us at Our Open Day")

|     |
| --- |
| **Only students who will result eligible for the ISU Bocconi Scholarship a.y. 2026-27 will be entitled to participate to the Open reservation — recovery session (preferential rate)** |

**1\. Housing rate change for a.y. 2026-27**
