metadata.heading_path: Open reservation - recovery session a.y 2026-27 - Bocconi University

text:
This housing application opportunity adds to the others foreseen for a.a. 2026-27:
- [Dedicated to Freshmen - Olympic Village](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/dedicated-freshmen "Dedicated to Freshmen")
- [Full rate Bocconi residence halls](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/full-rate-ay-2026-27 "Full rate a.y. 2026-27") \- open to all Bocconi students
Application available from 17 to 24 February 2026.

Only for students who are already guests at preferential rate as of 17 February 2026.

[Online application](https://admissions.unibocconi.it/admissions/Students/OpenFromExtUrl?concourseId=14358&ordType=)

Application available from 17 to 24 February 2026.

Attention: students enrolled for a.y. 2026-27 to the first year of a Bachelor, Integrated Master of Arts in Law or Master of Science program can apply for Preferential rate new admission application only after having submitted the “ISU Bocconi Scholarship a.y. 2026-27” application.

[Online application](https://admissions.unibocconi.it/admissions/Students/OpenFromExtUrl?concourseId=14359&ordType=)

Application available from 4 to 8 November 2026

Only students who will result eligible for the ISU Bocconi Scholarship a.y. 2026-27 will be entitled to participate to the Open reservation — recovery session (preferential rate).

[Find out more](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/open-reservation-recovery-session-ay-2026-27)

Through the "Renewal" and the "New admission" application, rooms for the standard assignment period (late August 2026 — June 2027) will be assigned; this period meets the academic needs of most students.

Through the "Open reservation — recovery session" application, rooms at preferential rate will be assigned for the period January-June 2027.

Throughout the period in which the student is a resident in a Bocconi residence hall, room changes are not permitted. Instead, it is the right of Bocconi University to transfer the student, even during the year, to another residence hall at the rate provided for the new residence.

1\. Requirements

A preferential rate assignment may be requested only by students who:
- **Meet the economic requirements** established by the Lombardy Region for the access to the benefits of the Right to University Education for the a.y. 2026-27 and thenecessary documentation: please refer to the [**dedicated page**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/economic-requirements-submission-housing-application-ay-2026-27 "Economic requirements for the submission of housing application a.y. 2026-27") for all information. For the purpose of ascertaining the economic conditions and the truthfulness of the information provided, Bocconi University will make use of the faculties provided for by the current provisions of the law. Information on ascertaining the economic conditions and veracity of the statements provided is available at the following link.

- **are non-residents**, meaning living in a municipality from which it is possible to reach the location of the study course attended by public transportation, with travel times exceeding 90 minutes(\*)
The only exception are students interested in applying during the “Open reservation – recovery session”, for whom this requirement does not have to be strictly fulfilled;

metadata.title: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
- **do not fall under any of the** [**grounds for exclusion**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/grounds-exclusion-preferential-rate-ay-2026-27 "Grounds for exclusion - preferential rate a.y. 2026-27") **.**

- **in possession of the minimum number of credits required** to submit the application, detailed in the following point 2 "Credits required to submit the application"

- **will enroll for a.y. 2026-27:**

- for Bachelor programs: to the first, second, third or first "fuori corso" year of the third year

- for the Integrated Master of Arts in Law: to the first, second, third, fourth, fifth or first "fuori corso" year of the fifth year

- for Master of Science programs: to the first, second, or first "fuori corso" year of the second year

- **not already assignees of housing** **upon payment, or free of charge, or at a reduced rate for the academic year 2026-27.** In fact, students who have already been assigned housing and submit or have submitted other housing requests of any type for the same academic year may only benefit from the first housing assignment they accepted. Therefore, housing requests submitted by students who have already been assigned accommodation for the same academic year will be canceled.

metadata.title: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
**Students who are recipient of financial aid that includes** free **accommodation**(e.g., Una Scelta Possibile) or accommodation at reduced rate (e.g., Moving to Milan), upon acceptance of the benefit, renounce the possibility of receiving any another type of housing assignment (preferential rate, full rate) for the academic year of the first assignment and subsequent years (if the financial aid is renewed). Acceptance will result in the automatic cancellation of any housing request submitted and/or of any housing assignment received. It is also specified that after acceptance, no further housing requests of any type can be submitted.

The number of rooms available at preferential rate is usually lower than the applications submitted. Regularly enrolled students will be considered as a priority: applications submitted by students transferring from other Universities or repeating any year of course will not be accepted. Detailed rules on verifications carried out on years of enrollment are available in the Housing Application Requirements and Regulations a.y. 2026-27.

It is also specified, exclusively for students enrolled for a.y. 2026-27 to the first year of a Bachelor, Integrated Master of Arts in Law or Master of Science program **,** that they can apply for Preferential rate new admission application only after having submitted the “ISU Bocconi Scholarship a.y. 2026-27” application.

(\*) The travel times to be calculated are the minimum times required, based on the public transport route, between the departure station/stop from the place of residence and the arrival station/stop at the study location, including waiting times at intermediate stops. Walking routes or private transportation are excluded. These times can be verified on the regional website [www.muoversi.regione.lombardia.it](http://www.muoversi.regione.lombardia.it/) and, for cities outside the Lombardy Region, through the Google Maps search engine at [www.google.it/maps](http://www.google.it/maps)

2\. Credits required to submit the application

In the application sessions "Preferential rate renewal application" and "Preferential rate new admission application" will be evaluated only applications from students who have earned the number of credits specified below no later than: **3 February, 2026** for Bachelor and Master of Science programs, and by **25 March, 2026** for Integrated Master of Arts in Law program.

It is specified that:

>First year students of the Bachelor or Integrated Master of Arts in Law programs are not required to reach this minimum number of credits if they wish to apply for the "Preferential rate new admission application"

>A minimum number of credits is not required to students who wish to apply for the "Open reservation — recovery session" application.

**Minimum number of credits required**

**11 credits** for those enrolled in the first year (bachelor, master of science and integrated master of arts in law programs) in a.y. 2025-26

**60 credits** for those enrolled in the second year (bachelor, master of science or integrated master of arts in law programs) in a.y. 2025-26

**120 credits** for those enrolled in a.y. 2025-26 in the third year or first year “fuori corso” of a bachelor program who will enroll for a.y. 2026-27 in the first year of a master of science program at Bocconi, or those enrolled in a.y. 2025-26 in the third year of integrated master of arts in law programs

**180 credits** for those enrolled in the fourth year of an integrated master of arts in law program in a.y. 2025-26

**240 credits** for those enrolled in the fifth year of an integrated master of arts in law program in a.y. 2025-26

metadata.title: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
It is specified that for the achievement of the minimum number of credits required at the end of the winter session there is **NO use of bonus credits** in addition to the credits actually earned by the student.

For students who have participated in an Exchange program during the first semester, the timing of recognition of exams taken abroad will be considered. Such students are invited to still apply for housing renewal if interested and to monitor the credit recognition in order to complete it as soon as possible. Please note that no flexibility can be applied in relation to the achievement of the merit requirement for accommodation - credits to be achieved by August 10, 2026. (please see paragraph 5)

It is also specified that for students with disability pursuant to Italian Law n.104/1992 or with civil disability equal or greater than 66%, the number of minimum credits may deviate up to a maximum of 40% from the number of credits indicated above.

3\. Application submission

The housing assignment for A.Y. 2026-27 is granted for the academic year of application only, therefore, it is not automatically renewed for subsequent academic years, but it is the student's responsibility to check whether it can be renewed and to make subsequent applications for assignment in the ways that will be provided by the Housing Application Requirements and Regulations for each academic year of interest.

Throughout the period in which the student is a resident in a Bocconi residence hall, room changes are not permitted. Instead, it is the right of Bocconi University to transfer the student, even during the year, to another residence hall at the rate provided for the new residence.

Before accessing the online application:

>see the useful documents available at this [link](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/useful-documents "Useful documents")

>check the economic requirements and prepare the documentation required to access to the benefits of the Right to University Education for the a.y. 2026-27: details available on the [dedicated webpage](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/economic-requirements-submission-housing-application-ay-2026-27 "Economic requirements for the submission of housing application a.y. 2026-27")

**Preferential rate renewal application a.y. 2026-27**

**from 17 to 24 February 2026**

Preferential rate renewal application a.y. 2026-27

Available only for students who are already guests at preferential rate as of 17 February 2026.

If for A.Y. 2026-27 you are in possession of an ISEE/ISEEU parificato certificate with ISEE/ISEEU parificato and/or an ISPE/ISPEU parificato values above the required limits, you can apply for full rate housing renewal.

**ONLINE APPLICATION**

**Preferential rate new admission application a.y. 2026-27**

**from 17 to 24 February 2026**

Preferential rate new admission application a.y. a.y. 2026-27

**Attention**: students enrolled for a.y. 2026-27 to the first year of a Bachelor, Integrated Master of Arts in Law or Master of Science program **can apply for Preferential rate new admission application only after having submitted the “ISU Bocconi Scholarship a.y. 2026-27” application.**

metadata.title: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
Through this application will be assigned the remaining availability after the Preferential rate housing renewal application and within the number of beds defined on the Housing Application Requirements and Regulations A.Y. 2026-27 (understood as specific number of beds per single residence). Students who will be eligible for new admission at Preferential rate housing for A.Y. 2026-27 will be assigned housing. In case the number of applicants exceeds the number of available places, the assignment will be made according to the ranking list following the order described in the Housing Application Requirements and Regulations A.Y. 2026-27.

**ONLINE APPLICATION**

**Open reservation recovery session a.y. 2026-27**

**from 4 to 8 November 2026**

See the [dedicated webpage](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/open-reservation-recovery-session-ay-2026-27 "Open reservation - recovery session a.y 2026-27") for more information

Applications submitted after the deadline and/or with submission methods that do not comply with the prescribed procedure and/or with incomplete documentation will not be taken into consideration.

After submitting your application, monitor in the "Notifications" area of MyApplication for communications from the Office, which may indicate the need for additions or integrations of documents.

**How to upload the documents correctly**

You must print all documents, sign them by hand with a legible signature, and upload them with all pages to the designated section no later than the application’s deadline. In order to complete the application, it is necessary to click on the button "submit application" after having completed the 600 euros housing deposit payment and the upload of all the documents required, that are available in the online application. The signature on the documents must be handwritten and match the signature on the ID submitted.

4\. Results and acceptance

**Preferential rate renewal application result**

The result will be made available within early April2026 on MyApplication. For the renewal application, no subsequent steps of acceptance are required, the room is assigned.

**Preferential rate new admission application result and acceptance**

The result will be made available within early April 2026 on MyApplication.

If the result is positive, you will receive a preferential rate housing assignment for A.Y. 2026-27. On the result, you will be notified the residence assigned, the room type, the period of assignment and the applicable Bocconi ISU bracket, which may change following the Lombardy Region's resolution on the final ISEEU/ISPEU parameters. The assignment offer received cannot be changed and no room changes are expected.

If you wish to accept the offer of accommodation received, proceed to pay the security deposit of 600 euros and upload all the required documents by the date that will be indicated in the result. If you fail to complete all the steps required to accept the housing assignment received by that deadline, your assignment will automatically be cancelled.

**Open reservation recovery session result and acceptance**

The result will be made available in the month of November 2026 on MyApplication.

Rate change

If the result is positive, you will receive a preferential rate housing assignment for A.Y. 2026-27. On the result, you will be notified the residence assigned, the room type, the period of assignment and the applicable Bocconi ISU bracket. No subsequent steps of acceptance are required.

In the event of a request of housing rate change with a negative result due to non-availability of assignable housing at preferential rate (as specified above), the student may apply for withdrawal by and no later than November 2026.

New admissions

metadata.title: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
If the result is positive, you will receive a preferential rate housing assignment for A.Y. 2026-27. On the result, you will be notified the residence assigned, the room type, the period of assignment and the applicable Bocconi ISU bracket. The assignment offer received cannot be changed and no room changes are expected.

If you wish to accept the offer of accommodation received, proceed to pay the security deposit of 600 euros and upload all the required documents by the date that will be indicated in the result.

If you fail to complete all the steps required to accept the housing assignment received by that deadline, your assignment will automatically be cancelled.

In the event of a preferential rate new admission housing application (recovery session) with a negative result due to non-availability of assignable housing at preferential rate, the student may apply for the Open reservation at full rate (during the first session in December 2026).

5\. Requirements needed to use the accommodation

Students who will result housing assignees for a.y. 2026-27 following the procedure described above will be able to use the assigned accommodation only in presence of the following conditions:

**1) REGULAR ENROLLMENT for a.y. 2026-27**

\> regular enrollment for the absolute first time for a.y. 2026-27 in the first regular year of a Master of Science (i.e. bachelor Bocconi students going to the Master of Science program)

or

\> regular enrollment for a.y. 2026-27 for the absolute first time in a regular year subsequent to the first of a Bachelor of Science, Master of Science, Integrated Master of arts in Law or PhD program. Also students enrolled in the 1st year “fuori corso” of a Bachelor of Science, Master of Science, Integrated Master of Arts in Law program will be able to reside in the Bocconi residence halls.

It is specified that the years of enrollment are counted for each study cycle starting from the absolute first year of enrollment\*.

It is specified that for students with a disability pursuant to Italian Law n.104/1992 or with civil disability equal or greater than 66% can reside in the Bocconi Residence Halls at a preferential rate also if enrolled for a.y. 2025-26 in the 2nd year “fuori corso” year of a Bachelor of Science, Master of Science, Integrated Master of Arts in Law program.

\*The absolute first year of enrollment shall be understood as any academic year of first enrollment in any kind of degree program (even if different from the one attended in the a.y. 2026-27) which required the same education qualification as the one needed to access the attended program in the a.y. 2026-27, in any University in Italy or abroad, even if the program has not been completed.

**2) MERIT REQUIREMENT**

For the purpose of eligibility to use the accommodation, the rules established in terms of merit requirements and detailed on the [**dedicated webpage**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/merit-requirements-ay-2026-27 "Merit requirements a.y. 2026-27") for the different types of housing applications apply.

6\. Payment

Before your arrival at the residence for a.y. 2026-27 you will have to pay the housing 1st installment: the deadline will be August 5th, 2026. You will see the amount due on your you@B once you'll have completed your enrollment for a.y. 2026-27. We recommend you to make the payment through the **Flywire tool** system.

metadata.title: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
[**Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2026-27**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/fees-deadlines-and-methods-payment-ay-2026-27 "Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2026-27")

Please consider that the calculation of the housing installment is carried out at the beginning of July for all housing assignees, regardless of their situation in terms of reaching the merit and the enrollment requirement (see point 5) that the office cannot verify before August 10. The possibility of seeing the due payment on you@B does **not** entail an unconditional room assignment, which is always subject to the possession of the enrollment and merit requirements. In absence of the requirements needed to use the assigned room, the office will proceed with the cancellation of your housing assignment, but you will still be required to pay the first installment (it will not be possible to cancel the due amount).

In the event you will pay the amount due but after a subsequent verification the merit and enrollment requirement will not be met, the amount you paid will not be refunded and the housing assignment will be cancelled in any case.

Please remember that the University grants the right to leave the accommodation early and to withdraw from the commitment to such payment, exclusively in the foreseen cases of renunciation of the accommodation, proven by the specific academic reasons required for the acceptance of the renunciation request, and withdrawal from the accommodation, according to what detailed on the dedicated website pages.

7\. Cancellation in case of lack of the requirements

Students assigned housing for whom it is subsequently determined that they did not regularly enroll for the a.y. 2026-27 or did not meet the merit requirements will lose their right (eligibility) to use the Housing Service for the a.y. 2026-27. The assignment will be cancelled by the office and students will be required to leave the assigned accommodation immediately. It is specified that the payments due will remain the responsibility of the student even in the event of cancellation of the assignment. For this reason, before submitting a housing application, students are invited to pay particular attention to the provisions regarding the cancellation of the housing assignment, specified on the [**dedicated webpage**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/cancellation-and-revocation-ay-2026-27 "Cancellation and Revocation a.y. 2026-27").

8\. Residence halls calendar and extraordinary openings

Bocconi residences will be open from late August 2026 to late June 2027. The standard assignment period for the 2025-26 academic year will meet the academic needs of most students.

There are no time restrictions for entry or exit for residence guests. However, there are restrictions for external guests, whose presence in the residence is allowed exclusively between 7:00 AM and 12:00 AM.

**Important check-out note**

Regardless of the end date of your housing assignment, we remind you that **at the end of your housing assignment period, you must completely clear your room and apartment of all your belongings**, even if you will return to the same room the following year. In fact, as specified in the Bocconi Residences Regulations:

_At the time of check-out for the end of the assignment period or during the year, the student is required to completely empty their room and other areas of their accommodation (bathroom, kitchen, living room, where present), including completely emptying refrigerators and freezers. Bocconi University is not responsible for goods left in the apartment/room after check-out._

metadata.title: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Preferential rate a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
Attention: rooms are reassigned to Summer School students during the summer, so it is not possible to leave anything inside your room or apartment. **Any issues related to missing or deteriorated items during the summer, a period when the room is not assigned, cannot be considered** by Bocconi University or the residence management.

If necessary, we suggest you consider luggage storage services that are very common in Milan, such as Boxok, Redbox Storage, Radical Storage, and Casaforte Milano.

Regarding stays during extraordinary opening periods (Winter break 2026 and July 2027), a separate request will be necessary (information available soon).

9\. Students with disabilities

Bocconi University has accommodations in each residence hall that are designed to accommodate students with disabilities in order to offer them the possibility to easily live their university experience in a collective context that meets their needs. Students with disabilities who require special housing needs may contact the Office of Fees, Funding and Housing, which will evaluate, with each student, which housing is best suited to their specific needs. All reported issues and provided documentation will be treated confidentially and subject to applicable privacy regulations. In order for the Office to be able to satisfy the requests received, it is best to clarify your needs in advance. Interested students are advised to contact the Fees, Funding and Housing Office before submitting their housing application.

The preferential rate for the accommodation service can only be applied to students who are in need but deserving and who meet the requirements (in terms of merit and income) for the ISU Bocconi Scholarship for the Right to University Education. For the preferential rate, 3 different fee levels based on the ISU brackets (1st, 2nd and 3rd ISU bracket) are foreseen, depending on the values indicated on the ISEE/ISEEU Parificato Certificate submitted at the moment of the application. For all other students the full rate is applied.

On this webpage, all the details about requirements, methods and deadlines of application, results and requirements needed to use the accommodation are explained.

Before applying, be sure to carefully read the contents of paragraphs 1-9 on this page.

You can access the application you are interested in through the boxes at the top of the page; links will be available during the application period.

Applying to Bocconi

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi)

Bachelor and Law Programs

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs)

Bocconi Residence Halls

_The content of this page applies exclusively to the 7 Bocconi Residence Halls. The partner facilities Aparto Giovenale and Olympic Village follow specific regulations and policies shared by their respective managers._

#### **Renunciation**

It is possible to renounce from the housing assignment exclusively for expected academic reasons and only within the time windows detailed below.

**Renunciation from June 3-7, 2026  – only for students who:**

Have received confirmation of departure for exchange or internship for a.y. 2026-27.

**The application will be immediately accepted and the room assigned for 2026-27 will be cancelled.**

The office will verify afterwards with the competent offices the truthfulness of the statement, in case of substantiated statements the security deposit will be refunded and the student will not be burdened with penalties or further payments.

**Please note: In case of declarations NOT substantiated by the specific academic reasons provided for the acceptance of the renunciation request, the student will not be refunded the security deposit, will still have the obligation to pay one month's fees and will also be subject to the payment of 200 euros for administrative management charges**.

Please note: Students who fall under the categories for subsequent windows (e.g., lack of credits or withdrawal from studies) and are already certain they want to give up their accommodation can also submit a withdrawal request during this window.

**Renunciation from June 15-21, 2026** **– only for students enrolled in a Bachelor or Master’s program who:**

a) Have realized that they will not achieve the credits required for the merit requirement by 10 August, 2026;

b) Withdraw from their studies or transfer to another university;

c) They are Bachelor students and, despite having participated in the selections for admission to a Master’s program for the a.y. 2026-27, were not admitted. Please note: Bachelor students who are guests at a preferential rate and are not admitted to a Master’s program, but will enroll in the first year fuori corso, will not be able to submit a renunciation request.

**The application will be immediately accepted and the room assigned for 2026-27 will be cancelled.**

The office will verify afterwards with the competent offices the truthfulness of the statement, in case of substantiated statements the security deposit will be refunded and the student will not be burdened with penalties or further payments.

**Please note: In case of declarations NOT substantiated by the specific academic reasons provided for the acceptance of the renunciation request, the student will not be refunded the security deposit, will still have the obligation to pay one month's fees and will also be subject to the payment of 200 euros for administrative management charges**.

**Renunciation from July 6-12, 2026  - only for students with exams taken after the previous renunciation window deadline:**

Have realized that they will not achieve the credits required for the merit requirement by 10 August, 2026.

**The application will be immediately accepted and the room assigned for 2026-27 will be cancelled.**

The office will verify afterwards with the competent offices the truthfulness of the statement, in case of substantiated statements the security deposit will be refunded and the student will not be burdened with penalties or further payments.

metadata.title: Renunciation and Withdrawal a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Renunciation and Withdrawal a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
**Please note: In case of declarations NOT substantiated by the specific academic reasons provided for the acceptance of the renunciation request, the student will not be refunded the security deposit, will still have the obligation to pay one month's fees and will also be subject to the payment of 200 euros for administrative management charges**.

#### **Withdrawal**

You may withdraw from the housing assignment by submitting a withdrawal application during the month of **November, 2026**(dates soon available) and **exclusively if you fall into one of the two cases** listed below:

\> during the second semester of a.y. 2026-27 you will participate in an international **mobility** experience (exchange, free mover) or an **internship** recognized by Bocconi University, in a location other than Milan;

\> you participated in the Open reservation - recovery session because you were already assigned a full rate accommodation and you wanted to **change the rate**, switching to the preferential rate, but you received a **negative result**. In this case, the deadline for submitting the withdrawal request will be communicated to you in the result of the Open reservation - recovery session.

There are no other cases of withdrawal; in all other cases, the student will keep the housing assignment for the entire agreed period and will be required to pay all the scheduled housing installments.

Application for withdrawal results in cancellation of the housing assignment **starting from December 24, 2026**. If you fall into this category, you will not be required to pay subsequent installments and will get a refund of the security deposit.

**WITHDRAWAL APPLICATION**

Available during the application period

Applying to Bocconi

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi)

Bachelor and Law Programs

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs)


The housing assignment will be revoked in the following cases:

**a. Disciplinary Procedures**

A student who receives a disciplinary sanction of exclusion from Università Bocconi during the year incurs **revocation of the housing assignment with consequent expulsion from the residence hall** and loss of the security deposit. In this case the student will have to permanently leave the housing assigned to him/her.

A student who receives a disciplinary sanction of suspension from exams and partial exams during the year will not incur revocation of the housing assignment, however, if he or she has an assignment at preferential rate, he or she will undergo the **change of rate from preferential to full rate**, and will therefore be required to pay the housing fee at the full rate starting from the first installment due after the sanction is communicated.

**b. Serious violation of the Bocconi Residence Regulations**

A student who during the course of the year incurs disciplinary sanctions in excess of a warning imposed by the university incurs **revocation of the housing assignment resulting in the expulsion from the residence hall** and loss of the security deposit. In this case, the student must permanently leave the housing assigned. The university reserves the right to refer the student to the Disciplinary Committee. The university also reserves the right in this case to order the ineligibility of the student to use the Housing Service for the entire duration of the study cycle.

**c. False declarations**

A student who submits/has submitted/ untrue statements incurs **revocation of the housing assignment resulting in expulsion from the residence hall** and loss of the security deposit. In this case, the student must immediately and permanently leave the housing assigned. The sanctions provided for in Articles 75 and 76 of Presidential Decree 445/2000 will also be applied, as well as the sanctions provided for in Article 10, paragraph 3 of Legislative Decree 68/2012 consisting in the loss of the right to obtain other disbursements for the entire duration of the course of studies, the payment of a sum three times the value of the services unduly used, without prejudice in any case to the application of the sanctions provided for in Article 38, paragraph 3, of Legislative Decree No. 78/2010 as well as the criminal provisions for facts constituting a crime.

Università Bocconi reserves the right to adopt disciplinary sanctions and, if the elements of a crime are found to exist (art. 331 c.p.p.), reporting to the judicial authorities.

[VIALE GIOVANNI SPADOLINI 12/A](https://www.google.it/maps/place/Residenza+Spadolini/@45.4424414,9.1910169,17z/data=%214m13%211m7%213m6%211s0x4786c413f2197f0b:0x4941cc992861928f%212sVia+Giovanni+Spadolini,+12,+20141+Milano+MI%213b1%218m2%213d45.4424377%214d9.1932056%213m4%211s0x4786c413f8edd4bb:0xee5592beb09a67c9%218m2%213d45.4429935%214d9.1941646)

Located just a 10-minute walk from campus, the **Spadolini Residence** has:

**A.** 317 single rooms in 3/4-bedroom apartments with 2 bathrooms, a shared living room/kitchen area and a balcony.

**B.** 16 studio apartments with a bedroom, bathroom, living room/kitchen area and a balcony.

**Description of the spaces**

All rooms are furnished with a bed, a desk, a wardrobe and are equipped with WiFi internet. Blankets, sheets, pillowcases and towels are provided. Linens are changed and rooms are cleaned once a week. Kitchens are located in the common areas of the 4-bedroom apartments or in the studio apartments. In addition, there are study rooms, break rooms, a coin laundry area and a relax area.

metadata.title: Spadolini Residence | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Spadolini Residence | Bocconi University

text:
## Dorm amenities


### Wifi


### Laundry


### Study area


### Recreation area


### Paid parking

Why live in University housing?

First of all, **location**: university residence halls are on or near campus, close to classes and the university facilities, and just minutes from the center of town.

Another important advantage of dorm life is constant **interaction** with your fellow students, Italian and international alike. You'll meet lots of different people with whom you'll have many things in common - interests, ambitions and dreams - in addition to your studies. It will be friendly environment where you'll soon feel at home.

Only students who are attending Bocconi University or participating in exchange programs are allowed to access the residence halls.

Plus, living in one of the university residence halls is both **safe and convenient**. All our residence halls have a doorman ready to help you 24/7, and the services included in your lodging fee will allow you to maintain your busy schedule without worrying about the electric bill or changing the sheets.

All Bocconi residence halls are closed during summer period, according to the period of suspension of courses and exams.

What the residents love about the Spadolini Residence

- The Spadolini Residence is located 10-minute walk from the campus;
- The area is well served with pharmacies, supermarkets and shops; it is also close to public transport to reach the city center;
- It is located close to green areas and parks.

How to book a room?

**Booking a room in a Bocconi residence is simple**: participate in one of the online applications planned for the 2026-27 academic year, at preferential or full rate. Please check the information available on our homepage at the following [link](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing "Housing").

[Join Us at Our Open Day](https://bocconiopenday.liveforum.space/membership/login?lang=en-GB&source=float_login "Join Us at Our Open Day")

text:
Please note that:
- In case of rooms with shared bathroom, it is necessary that the subleasing student is of the same gender of the student who sublets their room.

- The room can be temporarily sublet only for full months.

- For every temporary sublease request, a fee equal to 100,00 euros will be applied to each student (outgoing and incoming)

- According to the agreement in place among the student who is temporary leaving the room and the subleasing student, it will be possible to sublet the room for one or more consecutivemonths, as long as they belong to the same semester (i.e. the period from September to January for the 1° semester and the period from January to June for the 2° semester). The participation in the international program will be verified by the competent offices.

- The student who will temporarily occupy the room will not have to meet economic or merit requirements.
Housing fee calculation in case of temporary sublease

With reference to the due housing fees in case of temporary sublease, it is specified that:
- For students involved in the temporary sublease, the housing fees will be determined according to the monthly fee.

- Should the room be at the **FULL RATE**, the student who will temporary occupy the room will pay for the accommodation exclusively at full rate (monthly fee).
Should the room be at **PREFERENTIAL RATE**, the student who will temporary occupy the room will pay for the accommodation at full rate except in one of the following cases:
- The student who will temporarily occupy the room have applied for “preferential rate new admission a.y. 2026-27” (results in April 2026) and had a housing assignment (later not accepted by themselves) or a non-housing assignment due to lack of places (note1). In these cases, at the time of the sublease/take over request, the student concerned may request to pay at the PREFERENTIAL rate.
or
- Should the room be at **preferential rate**, the student who will temporary occupy the room will be eligible for this rate only in the event that they have applied for the "ISU Bocconi Scholarship a.y. 2026-27" and that, following the result announcement of the final ranking (by the end of October 2026), they have been found eligible for the ISU Bocconi scholarship and has thus been assigned one of the ISU Bocconi brackets. In this case it is specified that the preferential rate will be applied only to the installments expected to expire AFTER the definition of its eligibility. If the incoming person has to pay an installment before the publication of the results of the ISU rankings - therefore who has agreed to take over for a period between September and December - he will have to pay the full rate.
**Note1**

The students received the following result: “we regret to inform you that is was NOT possible to assign you a room at preferential rate in one of our Bocconi on campus residences for the a.y. 2026-27 as the number of applicants exceeded the number of available spots reserved for the right to university education assistance a.y. 2026-27”.

Application submission

Temporary sublease applications can be submitted only by 2026-27 room assignees who meet the merit and/or economic and enrollment requirements as defined for housing applications.

In case of non-compliance with one or more requirements and subsequent revocation of housing assignment, the temporary sublease application will NOT be considered valid.

The student who sublets their room is responsible for any missing payments and, should that be the case, they will be required to reimburse the University.

The temporary sublease application will need to be submitted through the online procedure that can be accessed from the "Links" box, and only during the following periods:

metadata.title: Temporary sublease a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Temporary sublease a.y. 2026-27 | Bocconi University

text:
- **From June 3-7, 2026** for students who will take part in the international program during the I or II semester a.y. 2026-27;

- **October 2026** for students who will take part in the international program during the II semester a.y. 2025-26.
Temporary sublease application – **FIRST** semester a.y. 2026-27

PLEASE NOTE: Both the Outgoing and the Incoming are required to complete their dedicated application. Should one of the two applications not be submitted, the Temporary Sublease request will be rejected.

ONLINE APPLICATION – OUTGOING STUDENTS (First Semester)

ONLINE APPLICATION – INCOMING STUDENTS (First Semester)

Temporary sublease application – **SECOND** semester a.y. 2026-27

PLEASE NOTE: Both the Outgoing and the Incoming are required to complete their dedicated application. Should one of the two applications not be submitted, the Temporary Sublease request will be rejected.

ONLINE APPLICATION – OUTGOING STUDENTS (Second Semester)

ONLINE APPLICATION – INCOMING STUDENTS (Second Semester)

Role of the office

Temporary sublease agreements are **supervised** by Fees, Funding and Housing Office **only** from an administrative point of view (collection and verification of the documentation, issue of the relevant period MAV payments, checks with the competent offices) and organizational point of view (communication with Bocconi residences concerning students involved and period of assignment) in order to facilitate and support students taking part in mobility programs or students looking for a temporary accommodation.

If a **shared need of modification** of the sublease agreement emerges after the deal is made (ex. extension or reduction of the period of stay), the students involved in the temporary sublease must jointly and in agreement with each other contact the Office, who will consider the request and carry out the necessary administrative procedures.

On the contrary, should any misunderstanding or conflicting needs arise between the students involved in the sublease, the Office will **NOT** intervene, and only the original agreement, formerly confirmed by the Office, will be considered as valid. The Office will intervene with the necessary administrative steps just in case the students involved in the sublease will find a mutual agreement among themselves, and when they will communicate to the Office the **modification request jointly and in a written form.**

Please note that it is **NOT** possible to cancel the temporary sublease agreement.

Periods and payments
- The period must be agreed between you and must correspond to that of the international experience; it will be possible to sublet the room for one or more consecutive months, as long as they belong to the same semester (i.e. the period from September to January for the 1° semester and the period from January to June for the 2° semester).

- It will be possible to sublet your room also for **the entire academic year,** submitting two separate temporary sublease applications – the first in June, the second in October/November.

- Each student will pay the monthly rate for the type of room for their actual months of stay, receiving the MAV in the you@B diary.
**Note**: temporary sublease will only be possible if the student participates in an international program in person; requests cannot be submitted if the program is held online.

If you will be assigned a room in one of the Bocconi residence Halls for a.y. 2026-27 and during the same academic year you will take part in the Exchange program, Free Mover or will participate in an off campus internship recognized by Bocconi University (verified by the competent offices), you can ask to temporarily sublet your room to another Bocconi student, who must be **regularly enrolled in the a.y. 2026-27** in a Bachelor, Master of Science or Integrated Master of Arts in Law program, in correspondence of the period during which you will take part in the international experience.

It is in student responsibility to find and indicate the name of a Bocconi student interested in temporarily occupying your room. We suggest you to visit the [**link**](https://www.facebook.com/groups/187502362158325) to the unofficial Facebook group handled by student representatives, that may be useful to find another Bocconi student interested in a temporary room assignment.

Applying to Bocconi

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi)

Bachelor and Law Programs

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs)

For the purposes of ascertaining an applicant’s financial status, ISU Bocconi – the Student Assistance and Financial Aid Center will avail itself of the means provided for by law, in particular article, 10 of Italian D.lgs. n.68/2012, article 71 of Italian D.P.R. 445/2000, article 11 del DCPM 159/2013 and INPS memorandum no. 171/2014, and at any time may request supporting documentation attesting to the veracity of the declaration verifying the truthfulness, consistency and correspondence of the economic and financial situation resulting from the comparison between the ISEE/ISEEU parificato certificate, the additional information and documentation provided and the documentation and information in its records. For the purpose of this verification, the University may also request, at any time, any additional documentation useful for verifying the truthfulness of the information, declarations and documentation submitted.

By agreement with the Lombardy Region Revenue Office, Ministry of Finance, the accuracy of the self-certification provided by students who are deemed eligible for financial services or provision of services through open competition will be checked, pursuant to current laws and in particular article 71 of ItalianPresidential Decree 445/2000.Furthermore, information regarding ISU beneficiaries will be sent to the National Institute of Social Security and to the Department of Labor and Social Policy according to Govt. Decree no. 78 of 31 May 2010.

Furthermore, information regarding ISU beneficiaries will be sent to the National Institute of Social Security and to the Department of Labor and Social Policy according to Govt. Decree no. 78 of 31 May 2010. If an applicant's declaration is false, sanctions will be applied pursuant to articles 75 and 76 of Italian Presidential Decree 445/2000 in addition to the sanctions foreseen by Art. 10 contained in Italian Law 68/2012 and in particular:

1\. In the delay of implementation of regulations referred to in article 38, paragraph 2, of the Italian Law-Decree no. 78 of 31 May 2010, converted, with changes from Italian Law no. 122 of 30 July 2010, organizations referred to in article 3, paragraph 1, check the veracity of the family situation declared by the student by comparing the income and asset information declared by recipients of actions with the information in possession of the Internal Revenue Service IT system. For that end, universities, higher education artistic, musical and dance institutions and service provider organizations are given the power to directly access the Interexchange Tax Registry System of Local Organizations (SIATEL) at the Internal Revenue Service, upon stipulation of the appropriate agreement.

2\. Service provider organizations send the lists of recipients of the same Financial Administration and can request them to carry out fiscal verifications and checks. The members of the family household of students benefiting from services are included in the categories that they are subject to, pursuant to applicable regulations, with maximum controls.

metadata.title: Verification of Financial Status and Validation of Economic Information and Documentation Provided | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Verification of Financial Status and Validation of Economic Information and Documentation Provided | Bocconi University

text:
3\. Without finding themselves in the conditions established by the state and regional regulations, anyone who submits false declarations, either regarding themselves or members of their family household, in order to make use of related actions, is subject to an administrative sanction consisting in the payment of a sum equal to triple the benefit unduly received, and loses the right to obtain other grants for the duration of the study program, without prejudice to in any case the application of sanctions referred to in article 38, paragraph 3 of the Italian Law-Decree no. 78 of 31 May 2010, converted, with changes from Italian Law no. 122 of 30 July 2010, as well as criminal laws for the actions making up the crime.

4\. Pursuant to the aforementioned laws, the treatment of the data acquired through the application for benefit assignment, with the purpose of verifying the economic conditions and the veracity of the situation of the student’s family household, is necessary in compliance with a legal obligation pursuant to article 10 of Italian D.lgs. 68/2012, article 71 of Italian D.P.R. 445/2000 and article 11 of Italian DPCM 159/2013, as well as for the performance of the University tasks carried out in the public interest as an academic institution, in compliance with article 34, paragraphs 3 and 4 of the Constitution, with DPCM 9 April 2001 and with Italian D.Lgs. 68/2012. The treatment of sensitive data communicated through the application for benefit assignment is necessary for the performance of the University tasks carried out in the public interest in compliance with DPCM 9 April 2001 and Italian D.Lgs. 68/2012 (articles 6 and 9 of the General Data Protection Regulation no. 679/2016).

Pursuant to article 2947 of the Italian Civil Code, checks will be carried out within 5 years starting from the last undue use and resulting from self-certification that was revealed to be false.

Bocconi University reserves the right to implement disciplinary sanctions and, if details regarding a crime are recognized (article 331 of Italian CPP), report it to legal authorities.

You may withdraw from the housing assignment by submitting a withdrawal application from **November 5 - 9, 2025**, and **exclusively if you fall into one of the two cases** listed below:

\> during the second semester of a.y. 2025-26 you will participate in an international **mobility** experience (exchange, free mover) or an **internship** recognized by Bocconi University, in a location other than Milan;

\> you participated in the Open reservation - recovery session because you were already assigned a full rate accommodation and you wanted to **change the rate**, switching to the preferential rate, but you received a **negative result**. In this case, the deadline for submitting the withdrawal request will be communicated to you in the result of the Open reservation - recovery session.

There are no other cases of withdrawal; in all other cases, the student will keep the housing assignment for the entire agreed period and will be required to pay all the scheduled housing installments.

Application for withdrawal results in cancellation of the housing assignment **starting from December 24, 2025**. If you fall into this category, you will not be required to pay subsequent installments and will get a refund of the security deposit.

**WITHDRAWAL APPLICATION**

Available from 5 to 9 November 2025

a.y. 2026-27

#### Your space, at the heart of the university experience

Living at Bocconi: 70 years of Investment in student growth and talent

For 70 years, Bocconi University has provided housing for its out-of-town students. Relocation to Milan has always been a central element of the University’s vision, which is firmly committed to supporting a steadily growing number of families by offering high-quality, safe, and inclusive housing solutions, while also developing accommodation opportunities designed to remain financially sustainable.

Over the years, Bocconi has expanded its portfolio to include seven University-owned residence halls, now able to accommodate up to 1,700 students. From the Bocconi Residence, inaugurated in 1956, to the more recent Castiglioni Residence, opened in 2018, the University has continuously invested in modern, energy-efficient facilities designed to welcome an increasingly international student community.

Starting in 2022, the housing offer was further expanded with an additional 350 beds, made possible through the University’s first partnership with an external organization.

In 2026, Bocconi once again reinforces its commitment to student support through housing, reserving approximately 700 new beds for its students at the prestigious and innovative Olympic Village. This state-of-the-art complex, inaugurated at the beginning of 2026 to host athletes competing in the Milan-Cortina 2026 Winter Olympic Games, will soon open its doors to the Bocconi community.

Within the Olympic Village, the University offers single and double rooms primarily **dedicated to first-year students**, many of whom are arriving in Milan for the first time to embark on an exciting journey of personal and academic growth.

Stories from our residents and families

“The Bligny Residence was more than just a home to me. It was the place where I met my friends and many classmates; the place where I studied, struggled, and celebrated, but above all where I grew and matured. I am grateful for the opportunity to have lived so many meaningful experiences there during such a delicate time in my life.”

“As a parent, knowing that my son was living on campus, in a well-maintained, secure facility under constant supervision, was essential. Over the years, I have always felt at ease: I knew he was in a protected, well-organized environment that genuinely cared about its residents, allowing him to experience university life with confidence and responsibility.”

text:
### Our housing offer

2800

Available beds

70+

Residents’ Nationalities

600+

Beds offered at particularly advantageous rates

Bocconi firmly believes in the **positive cross-fertilization** that arises from the encounter of diverse talents and has always invested in fostering it. Life within the University’s spaces is conceived as an experience of continuous exchange, where study, dialogue, and relationships intertwine every day, enriching each student’s academic and personal journey.

Living in a Bocconi residence means choosing a well-organized, reliable environment designed to **support students** throughout a stimulating and demanding university path. Functional, thoughtfully designed spaces are complemented by dedicated services and a strong sense of community that facilitates integration, encourages the development of lasting connections, and makes everyday life away from home smoother and more reassuring.

The University also strongly upholds the **principle of inclusion**, including from a financial perspective. It welcomes students from diverse backgrounds and offers them accommodation free of charge or at rates significantly below market value. In this way, every student—regardless of their background—can fully engage in the university experience, focusing on personal growth and the development of their talent.

First-year student – Resident at Spadolini Residence

“Spadolini was a vibrant place, shaped by study, exchange, and shared daily life. It was not just a room, but a space where I learned to manage my time, live with others, and build relationships that extended well beyond my university years.”

### Facilities

All the facilities available to our students offer well-maintained spaces, practical services, and an international community. They feature common areas, kitchens, and study rooms, and are all located in the heart of the city, within a maximum 10–15 minute walk from the classrooms.

**Bocconi Residences**

1. [Bligny Residence](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/bligny-residence)

**Our Partners**

**Olympic Village** – which hosted the athletes of the Milan-Cortina 2026 Winter Olympic Games. Designed to create a lasting legacy beyond the event, it will become one of the leading student housing hubs in Italy. **Starting from the end of August 2026, it will welcome Bocconi first-year students**, offering a safe and ideal environment for their first experience of university life, to be shared with their peers. The presence of numerous integrated services—including restaurants, shops, and gyms—will minimize everyday concerns and ensure both focus and comfort.

**Aparto Giovenale**, a Bocconi partner since 2022, located just a five-minute walk from the Bocconi campus, primarily reserved for exchange students spending a semester at Bocconi.

text:
To finalize your enrollment at Bocconi, if you have a non-EU citizenship, you are required to:
- apply for and obtain a **student visa (Type D)**, before coming to Italy;
- apply for a **"Permit of stay"** for study purposes, once in Italy.
How to apply for a student visa

Please be aware that you must be compliant with all immigration rules and regulations to complete your enrollment at Bocconi and come to Italy. Please visit [**http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en**](http://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/home/en) where a guided procedure will help you decide if you need to apply for a visa and where, based on your nationality, country of residence, reasons for your visit, and length of stay.

If you are **enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's of Science program**, regardless of your date of arrival in Milan, **you MUST submit your application on the Universitaly Portal by and no later than 30 September 2025.** However, we recommend that you apply as soon as you have all the documents required for the procedures.

**If you are enrolled in a Specialized Master's program,** you must submit your application via Universitaly according to the timeline of your program.

**If you are an incoming visiting student taking single courses,** you must follow the timeline of the semester you are attending.

In order to access the Universitaly portal, you need to:
1. Register if it’s your first access from: [https://universitaly-private.cineca.it/index.php/auth](https://universitaly-private.cineca.it/index.php/auth).
You will then receive an email after your registration with the Universitaly credentials (username and password) to access the portal.
2. Access the Universitaly portal from the following link: [https://universitaly-private.cineca.it/index.php/auth](https://universitaly-private.cineca.it/index.php/auth) with the username and password you have received after registration.
3. At first access, you will be asked to change your personal password. After changing the password, you will be able to access your **Reserved area** of the Universitaly portal
4. From here, you can click on the “Pre-enrolment application” link and again on the “New pre-enrollment application” green button
**For the registration, we remind you that you must enter your personal data exactly as shown on your passport with all your names and surnames.**

Then you will need to **fill out a 3-step form:**

**STEP A**:

Check the correctness of your **personal data and contacts,** and complete the missing information.

**STEP B:**

Fill out the form with your **passport number and expiration date**, then **upload a copy of your passport** (first 2 pages) and a passport-size picture of your face.

**Select the country** and the Italian Authority (Embassy or Consulate) where you will apply for your visa, then specify the **reason** why you are requesting a visa:
- For students enrolling in a Bachelor's, MSc, or Specialized Master's program, please select the option "I want to enroll in a study course".
- For incoming visiting students, please select the option "visiting students and free movers".
**Select the type of Institution (Università) and the Institution Name** ("Università Commerciale Luigi Bocconi"), then select the Course Type:

metadata.title: Immigration documentation | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Immigration documentation | Bocconi University

text:
- In case you are enrolled in a Bachelor's program, please select “Laurea”
- In case you are enrolled in an MSc program, please select “Laurea Magistrale”
- In case you are enrolled in a specialized Master's program, please select “Master universitario di primo livello” (I level) or “Master Universitario di secondo livello” (II level)
- In case you are an incoming Visiting student, please select “Corso/i singolo/i – Single course(s)”
**Enter your Bocconi ID account (it is a 7-digit Reference code – example: 3XXXXXX)**

**STEP C**:

Provide information on your **educational qualifications** and upload them to the system. Please note that in this section, you have to upload the same documents requested for your Bocconi enrollment application (see below). **We highly recommend that you start your application on Universitaly only when you already have all the documents required for the procedure.**

For students enrolled in a **BACHELOR PROGRAM**, these may include:
1. **Bocconi admission result** and a **Confirmation letter** that you can print out from the Bocconi Admissions Portal.
2. The last three years of **secondary school transcripts.**
3. **Final secondary school diploma and/or official proof of graduation.**
For students enrolled in a **MASTER OF SCIENCE PROGRAM**, these may include:
1. **Bocconi admission result** and **Confirmation letter** that you can print out from the Bocconi Admissions Portal.
2. Most updated **University transcripts.**
3. **Bachelor's diploma and/or official proof of graduation.**
4. **Diploma supplement** (if available).
For students enrolled in a **SPECIALIZED MASTER'S PROGRAM**, these may include:
1. **Bocconi admission result** and **Confirmation letter** that you can print out from the Bocconi Admissions Portal
2. Most updated **University transcripts**
3. **Bachelor's or Master of Science diploma**(according to the program of enrollment)
4. **Diploma supplement** (if available).
For students accepted as incoming **VISITING STUDENTS (SINGLE COURSES):**
1. **Official Admission results** that can be downloaded directly from the Bocconi Admissions Portal.
On the Universitaly portal, you will be asked to select the type of document you are uploading. If you cannot find the proper option, please select “Other” (for example: the Bocconi official Admission result, Confirmation letter).

In case you have been awarded with some sort of financial aid and/or accommodation from Bocconi _Fees, Funding & Housing Office,_ please upload the official result in the Universitaly application.

All documents are required **both in their original language and in English or Italian**. If the documents are not issued by the school/university in English or Italian, they should be legally translated.

Once you have submitted the application through the Universitaly Portal, the Admissions Office will verify the information you entered in the following weeks:
- In case some of the required documents are missing or your personal data is incorrect, you will be required to integrate the pre-enrollment application with the missing information and resubmit it.
- In case the information entered and the documents uploaded are correct, the application will be "validated" and sent to the competent Italian authority in your home country.

metadata.title: Immigration documentation | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Immigration documentation | Bocconi University

text:
Once the application has been validated, it will be sent to the Italian Authority you selected as in charge of your visa procedure. **After this step, you will receive a confirmation from the system, and only after that, you will be able to download the “Pre-enrollment receipts” directly from the Universitaly portal. You will then need to get in contact with the same Italian Authority to book your visa appointment** and to complete the pre-enrollment procedure. As a matter of fact, the student visa procedure does not consist only of the application to Universitaly, but it includes further steps to be completed at your Embassy/Consulate.

With reference to the need of providing a **Declaration of Value (DoV)**, please refer to the Admissions Office and/or the notifications in your enrollment application to verify if you have been asked to provide such document. Through your application on the Universitaly Portal, the Admissions Office will inform the Italian Authority in charge of your procedure on whether or not a DoV is requested. We remind you that as alternative to [DoV, the Statement of Comparability issued by CIMEA](https://www.cimea.it/EN/pagina-attestati-di-comparabilita-e-verifica-dei-titoli) is also accepted.

**We remind you that, as soon as you receive your student visa, you will be asked to provide a copy to the Admissions Office. This is compulsory to finalize your enrollment.**

**Without such a document, your enrollment will remain under “reserve”.**

**IMPORTANT**: For technical assistance on Universitaly, please contact the [**Help Desk**](https://mipa.support.cineca.it/support?token=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJmcm9tIjoid3d3LnVuaXZlcnNpdGFseS5pdCIsInNlc3Npb25faWQiOiI0MjRlMTYxNDM4MTYzMDNjZmY3NGZhMDZhYjliODc1ZCIsInNlcnZpY2VEZXNrSWQiOiIxNzAiLCJjb21wb25lbnRJZCI6IjU5NjM2IiwiZmFsbGJhY2tBZGRyZXNzIjoiamlyYS5zZG11bml2aXRAY2luZWNhLml0IiwicmVmZXJlciI6Imh0dHBzOlwvXC93d3cudW5pdmVyc2l0YWx5Lml0In0.zniHvyJD8dpe7VGv-ahuNyEJaTxJMwk1-zUeCubJP6Q).

How to apply for a permit of stay

**Within 8 working days from your arrival in Italy** holding a valid student visa, **you must apply for a permit of stay for study purposes** ("Permesso di soggiorno per motivi di studio").

The application for the permit of stay must be submitted at a competent Post Office. After the application, you will receive 1 or 2 payment receipts and a sheet with the date of your appointment at the local Police Office “Questura - Ufficio Stranieri”.

Further guidance about this procedure will be provided during the Welcome Days at the end of August 2025.

**Please note that, after the application at the Post Office, you are required to upload the receipts and the sheet with the appointment date at “Questura” in the dedicated section in your enrollment application. These documents are compulsory to finalize your enrollment.**

text:
For any further information: **[visa.support@unibocconi.it](mailto:visa.support@unibocconi.it)**

Declaration of Value

**DECLARATION OF VALUE FOR ENROLLMENT IN A BACHELOR OF SCIENCE PROGRAM**

The **Declaration of Value (DoV)** is an official document (written in Italian) that confirms the validity of the diploma, and it is issued by the competent Italian Authorities (Embassy/Consulate/Italian Cultural Institute) in the country to which the educational system of the school refers.

**When required**, to complete the enrollment, the DoV is a **compulsory** **condition for the secondary school diploma to be considered valid.**

The Declaration of Value must include the following information:

metadata.title: Immigration documentation | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Immigration documentation | Bocconi University

text:
- **legal status and nature of the awarding institution.**
- The final diploma has been **awarded after at least 12 years of education.**
- The diploma is v **alid to pursue studies in a 1st level degree program** in the country to which the educational system of your school refers (i.e., Bachelor programs).
- If required, **that you have passed the needed qualifying academic exam for university admission** (i.e., Gaokao, YKS, Panelladikés Exetáseis, Vestibular, etc.).
Admitted students are strongly recommended to get in touch with the Italian Authorities (Embassy/Consulate/Italian Cultural Institute) right after the admission for information about deadlines and procedures to request a valid DoV for the diploma.

In case the DoV is not required, non-EU applicants are still kindly invited to check anyway with the Italian Authorities (Embassy/Consulate/Italian Cultural Institute) in their country if a DoV is required for the Student Visa procedure.

The DoV must be provided to the Admissions Office **by no later than late August/early September 2025**.

Please note that Italian Embassies/Consulates do not submit any documents to the Italian Universities. Admitted students must submit the documents directly to Bocconi University through the enrollment application.

**DECLARATION OF VALUE FOR ENROLLMENT IN A MASTER OF SCIENCE PROGRAM**

The **Declaration of Value (DoV)** is an official document (written in Italian) that confirms the validity of the diploma issued by the competent Italian Authorities (Embassy/Consulate/Italian Cultural Institute) in the Country to which the educational system of your University refers.

If you are studying, for example, in a U.S. University in Europe or Asia, the Italian Authority in charge will be the territorially competent Italian Consulate/Embassy in the United States.

When specifically required in the admission “Result”, the Declaration of Value (DoV) is a compulsory condition for enrollment.

The Declaration of Value usually includes the following information:
- **legal status** and **nature** of the awarding institution: the University awarding the diploma is legally accredited in the Country to which the educational system of your undergraduate University refers (nationally valid in the Country);
- **requirements** needed for admission to the program ending in the qualification concerned;
- **legal duration** of studies (i.e., Bachelor program 3-4 years long) and global workload in hours/credits;
- **validity** of the qualification in the awarding country **for academic and/or professional ends**.

metadata.title: Immigration documentation | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Immigration documentation | Bocconi University

text:
If you are asked to provide the Admissions Office with the Declaration of Value, please note that this document **should be related to your undergraduate academic path (Bachelor level)** only and not to your Secondary School diploma, unless you are asked so by the competent Italian Authorities.

As an alternative to the Declaration of value, if the home University of the student has adhered to the so-called “Bologna Process”, we can evaluate to accept the “Diploma Supplement Document”.

The **Diploma Supplement** is not a further qualification, but a particular certification produced following a template that was agreed upon by the three principal international organizations of European breadth (The Council of Europe, UNESCO – Europe region, European Union). The European DS model envisages that it is drafted in two or more languages (the national language of the Country in question and at least one commonly-spoken foreign language, such as English) and that it provides a whole list of very detailed information.

The information is grouped into 8 categories, which run from the personal details of the qualification holder, to the level of the qualification itself, the curriculum (list of subjects and, if possible, their principal contents), the rights it bestows (utilization for further studies or in the workplace), to the type of issuing institution, and to an abbreviated description of the higher education system it belongs to.

We strongly recommend students submit the Declaration of Value/Diploma supplement to the Admissions Office as soon as they have it, uploading it on the My Application - Admission Portal **by the end of August/beginning of September 2025**.

Please note that a [**Statement of Comparability issued by CIMEA**](https://www.cimea.it/EN/pagina-attestati-di-comparabilita-e-verifica-dei-titoli) (the Italian NARIC Center) is considered a valid alternative to the declaration of value. The Statement of Comparability can be easily requested on the [**CIMEA website**](https://cimea.diplo-me.eu/unibocconi/#/auth/register).

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/immigration-documentation#addtoany "Show all")

[VIA BLIGNY 22](https://www.google.it/maps/place/Residenza+Bligny/@45.4509665,9.1874869,17z/data=%213m1%214b1%214m5%213m4%211s0x4786c404dfbf427f:0xf157cc6b887a8008%218m2%213d45.4509628%214d9.1896756)

Inaugurated in September 2016, the Bligny Residence has single rooms with a private bathroom or a bathroom shared with another student and a shared kitchen, and 4-bedroom apartments with 2 bathrooms and a shared living room/kitchen area and balcony.

**Description of the spaces**

All rooms are furnished with a bed, a desk, a wardrobe and are equipped with WiFi internet. Blankets, sheets, pillowcases and towels are provided. Linens are changed and rooms are cleaned once a week. Guests in Bligny Residence may use shared kitchens located in each floor or the kitchen located in the common areas of the apartments. In addition, there are study rooms, break rooms, a coin laundry area and a relax area.

text:
## Dorm amenities


### Wifi


### Laundry


### Study area


### Recreation area

Why live in University housing?

First of all, **location**: university residence halls are on or near campus, close to classes and the university facilities, and just minutes from the center of town.

Another important advantage of dorm life is constant **interaction** with your fellow students, Italian and international alike. You'll meet lots of different people with whom you'll have many things in common - interests, ambitions and dreams - in addition to your studies. It will be friendly environment where you'll soon feel at home.

Only students who are attending Bocconi University or participating in exchange programs are allowed to access the residence halls.

Plus, living in one of the university residence halls is both **safe and convenient**. All our residence halls have a doorman ready to help you 24/7, and the services included in your lodging fee will allow you to maintain your busy schedule without worrying about the electric bill or changing the sheets.

All Bocconi residence halls are closed during summer period, according to the period of suspension of courses and exams.

|
|

What the residents love about the Bligny Residence

- The Bligny Residence is located inside the campus;
- It is provided with large study rooms;
- It is located in a central area and well served by public transport.

How to book a room?

**Booking a room in a Bocconi residence is simple**: participate in one of the online applications planned for the 2026-27 academic year, at preferential or full rate. Please check the information available on our homepage at the following [link](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing "Housing").

### Campus VR

- [Take a Virtual Tour of the Residence](https://www.campusvr.unibocconi.it/en/Tour/Detail?id=2)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/master-science-and-ma-programs/housing/bligny-residence#addtoany "Show all")

It will be friendly environment where you'll soon feel at home.

Only students who are attending Bocconi University or participating in exchange programs are allowed to access the residence halls.

Plus, living in one of the university residence halls is both **safe and convenient**. All our residence halls have a doorman ready to help you 24/7, and the services included in your lodging fee will allow you to maintain your busy schedule without worrying about the electric bill or changing the sheets.

All Bocconi residence halls are closed during summer period, according to the period of suspension of courses and exams.

What the residents love about the Javotte Residence

- The Javotte Residence is the smallest and the quietest among the residences;
- It is provided with large areas and study rooms;
- It is located in a central area and well served by public transport.

How to book a room?

**Booking a room in a Bocconi residence is simple**: participate in one of the online applications planned for the 2026-27 academic year, at preferential or full rate. Please check the information available on our homepage at the following [link](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing "Housing").

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/master-science-and-ma-programs/housing/javotte-residence#addtoany "Show all")

[VIALE GIOVANNI SPADOLINI 12/A](https://www.google.it/maps/place/Residenza+Spadolini/@45.4424414,9.1910169,17z/data=%214m13%211m7%213m6%211s0x4786c413f2197f0b:0x4941cc992861928f%212sVia+Giovanni+Spadolini,+12,+20141+Milano+MI%213b1%218m2%213d45.4424377%214d9.1932056%213m4%211s0x4786c413f8edd4bb:0xee5592beb09a67c9%218m2%213d45.4429935%214d9.1941646)

Located just a 10-minute walk from campus, the Spadolini Residence has single rooms in 3/4-bedroom apartments with 2 bathrooms and a shared living room/kitchen, and studio apartments with bedroom, bathroom and living room/kitchen.

**Description of the spaces**

All rooms are furnished with a bed, a desk, a wardrobe and are equipped with WiFi internet. Blankets, sheets, pillowcases and towels are provided. Linens are changed and rooms are cleaned once a week. Kitchens are located in the common areas of the 4-bedroom apartments or in the studio apartments. In addition, there are study rooms, break rooms, a coin laundry area and a relax area.
