text:
Applying to Bocconi

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi)

Master of Science and MA Programs

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/master-science-and-ma-programs)



a.y. 2026-27

#### Your space, at the heart of the university experience

Living at Bocconi: 70 years of Investment in student growth and talent

For 70 years, Bocconi University has provided housing for its out-of-town students. Relocation to Milan has always been a central element of the University’s vision, which is firmly committed to supporting a steadily growing number of families by offering high-quality, safe, and inclusive housing solutions, while also developing accommodation opportunities designed to remain financially sustainable.

Over the years, Bocconi has expanded its portfolio to include seven University-owned residence halls, now able to accommodate up to 1,700 students. From the Bocconi Residence, inaugurated in 1956, to the more recent Castiglioni Residence, opened in 2018, the University has continuously invested in modern, energy-efficient facilities designed to welcome an increasingly international student community.

Starting in 2022, the housing offer was further expanded with an additional 350 beds, made possible through the University’s first partnership with an external organization.

In 2026, Bocconi once again reinforces its commitment to student support through housing, reserving approximately 700 new beds for its students at the prestigious and innovative Olympic Village. This state-of-the-art complex, inaugurated at the beginning of 2026 to host athletes competing in the Milan-Cortina 2026 Winter Olympic Games, will soon open its doors to the Bocconi community.

Within the Olympic Village, the University offers single and double rooms primarily **dedicated to first-year students**, many of whom are arriving in Milan for the first time to embark on an exciting journey of personal and academic growth.

Stories from our residents and families

“The Bligny Residence was more than just a home to me. It was the place where I met my friends and many classmates; the place where I studied, struggled, and celebrated, but above all where I grew and matured. I am grateful for the opportunity to have lived so many meaningful experiences there during such a delicate time in my life.”

“As a parent, knowing that my son was living on campus, in a well-maintained, secure facility under constant supervision, was essential. Over the years, I have always felt at ease: I knew he was in a protected, well-organized environment that genuinely cared about its residents, allowing him to experience university life with confidence and responsibility.”

metadata.title: Housing | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Housing | Bocconi University

text:
### Our housing offer

2800

Available beds

70+

Residents’ Nationalities

600+

Beds offered at particularly advantageous rates

Bocconi firmly believes in the **positive cross-fertilization** that arises from the encounter of diverse talents and has always invested in fostering it. Life within the University’s spaces is conceived as an experience of continuous exchange, where study, dialogue, and relationships intertwine every day, enriching each student’s academic and personal journey.

Living in a Bocconi residence means choosing a well-organized, reliable environment designed to **support students** throughout a stimulating and demanding university path. Functional, thoughtfully designed spaces are complemented by dedicated services and a strong sense of community that facilitates integration, encourages the development of lasting connections, and makes everyday life away from home smoother and more reassuring.

The University also strongly upholds the **principle of inclusion**, including from a financial perspective. It welcomes students from diverse backgrounds and offers them accommodation free of charge or at rates significantly below market value. In this way, every student—regardless of their background—can fully engage in the university experience, focusing on personal growth and the development of their talent.

First-year student – Resident at Spadolini Residence

“Spadolini was a vibrant place, shaped by study, exchange, and shared daily life. It was not just a room, but a space where I learned to manage my time, live with others, and build relationships that extended well beyond my university years.”

text:
### Facilities

All the facilities available to our students offer well-maintained spaces, practical services, and an international community. They feature common areas, kitchens, and study rooms, and are all located in the heart of the city, within a maximum 10–15 minute walk from the classrooms.

**Bocconi Residences**

1. [Bligny Residence](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/bligny-residence)
2. [Bocconi Residence](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/bocconi-residence)
3. [Castiglioni Residence](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/castiglioni-residence)
4. [Dubini Residence](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/dubini-residence)
5. I [sonzo Residence](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/isonzo-residence)
6. Ja [votte Residence](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/javotte-residence)
7. [Spadolini Residence](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/spadolini-residence)

**Our Partners**

**Olympic Village** – which hosted the athletes of the Milan-Cortina 2026 Winter Olympic Games. Designed to create a lasting legacy beyond the event, it will become one of the leading student housing hubs in Italy. **Starting from the end of August 2026, it will welcome Bocconi first-year students**, offering a safe and ideal environment for their first experience of university life, to be shared with their peers. The presence of numerous integrated services—including restaurants, shops, and gyms—will minimize everyday concerns and ensure both focus and comfort.

**Aparto Giovenale**, a Bocconi partner since 2022, located just a five-minute walk from the Bocconi campus, primarily reserved for exchange students spending a semester at Bocconi.

Campus

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus)

Buildings and Classrooms

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus/buildings-and-classrooms)


The **B in Touch Point** (former One-Stop Service Center) is a welcome point where staff are available who can offer you support to get started and direct you to the correct offices for your requests. In addition, at B in Touch Point you can pick up materials useful for your academic and campus life.

**Opening hours**

Mondays through Fridays from 8am to 5pm.

**How to reach us**

B in Touch Point is located in the Piazza Sraffa 11 building, with entrance at the back from Piazza Sraffa 13.

Information for prospective students

The Guidance and Recruitment Office is available for information on Bachelor and MSc programs and admissions procedures during On-Campus Infosessions:

- if you are interested in undergraduate programs, [click here](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/programs/bachelor-science/guidance-activities/campus-visits)
- if you are interested in graduate programs, [click here](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/master-science-programs/guidance-activities/campus-visits)

Information for current students

The **B in Touch** online service is a point of contact between students and University offices, and it is accessible directly from your **yoU@B student diary**. There you can find dedicated content for students and send requests to the various offices. [At this link](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=43ON-FlKBe4) you can watch a short clip on how to use the tool.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus/buildings-and-classrooms/b-touch-point#addtoany "Show all")

text:
From Linate Airport
- By taxi it takes about 20 minutes: more info on the [Linate airport website](https://milanolinate-airport.com/en/from-to/by-taxi).
- Subway line 4 (blue line) in the San Babila direction to Piazza Tricolore, then tram 9 (Porta Genova direction) to Via Bocconi.
From Malpensa Airport
- By taxi it takes about 50 minutes: more info on the [Malpensa airport website](https://www.milanomalpensa-airport.com/en/from-to/by-taxi).
- [Malpensa Express](https://www.malpensaexpress.it/en/) train to Cadorna Station, then subway line 2 (Abbiategrasso Chiesa Rossa or Assago Milanofiori Forum direction) to the Porta Genova stop, then tram 9 to Via Bocconi.
- [Malpensa Shuttle](https://www.malpensashuttle.it/) bus to Centrale Station, then subway line 3 (San Donato direction) to the Porta Romana stop, then tram 9 (Porta Genova direction) to Via Bocconi.
From Orio al Serio International Airport (Bergamo)

Information on the [Orio al Serio website](https://www.sacbo.it/Editorial/newsCategoryViewProcess.jsp?editorialID=171).

#### **By Train**

From Centrale Station
- By taxi it takes about 20 minutes.
- Subway line 3 in the San Donato direction to the Porta Romana stop, then tram 9 (Porta Genova direction) to Via Bocconi.
Suburban Rail Line S9 (Tibaldi stop)
- Signs for Bocconi are inside the station.
- Exit on Via Bazzi, cross the street to reach Via Castelbarco: access to the Bocconi Campus on Via Castelbarco and Viale Toscana, crossing the New Campus green.
#### **Taxi Service**

In Milan, taxi service is available 24 hours a day. Taxis can be the quickest way to get around the city because they often travel in dedicated lanes. You can request a taxi at the Bocconi reception, or call the numbers +39 02 4040 and +39 02 8585.

#### **Public Transport (Bus, Tram and Subway)**

Tickets for public transport cost €2.20 and are good for travel in the urban area and on the [Trenord](https://www.trenord.it/en/?_gl=1*dybndh*_up*MQ..*_ga*MTQ5OTA4Nzg0Ny4xNzQ2NTI2NDYx*_ga_CFQRRL9CP6*czE3NDY1MjY0NjAkbzEkZzAkdDE3NDY1MjY0NjAkajAkbDAkaDA.) urban rail network for 90 minutes after validation. Transfers are included, but with only one ride on subway and rail lines per ticket. For more information, visit the [ATM](https://www.atm.it/it/Pagine/default.aspx) (Azienda Trasporti Milanesi) website.

#### **By Car**

From the highway: Tangenziale Ovest Milano, exit Viale Liguria.

#### **Parking**

Quick Parking Service
- Bocconi "B" Via Röntgen 1
- Bocconi "C" Via Bocconi 8
- Bocconi "D" Via Castiglioni 8

text:
Campus

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus)


The new on-line procedure for determining one's professional level for the tax regime, which substitutes complicated paperwork, must be utilized by all external collaborators of any title (professionals, those with contracts similar to full-time amployees, and ongoing and temporary collaborators). In this way it is possible to provide one's personal data at any time from any place, adding and changing to it as needed.

With this procedure the Administration and Finance Service will have correct and easy-to-read data, making it easier to manage information and payments in a more efficient and timely manner.

The first on-line communication of personal data must be completed by all collaborators of the university starting from 1.1.2006; any changes to the data should be updated.

To access the on-line form you must obtain a username and password from the office (Institute or Research Centre) you work for. After first access, the program will ask you to provide a new username and personal password, which you can then use to update or check any personal data which previously enterd.

The interactive form will guide you through simple and quick steps as you fill out all personal, tax and social secutity data, as well as bank information, in order to have payments made to your account.

For ongoing and temporary collaborators (coordinati/continuativi and occasionali), the procedure requires two different stages for providing information. After the personal and bank information sections are filled out, the appropriate secretariat will send the request for collaboration (RdC) to the office involved, which will proceed to assign the collaboration into one of the two types listed above, according to the work entailed.

Once this is done, an e-mail is sent to the collaborator with a link to his or her personal homepage, where there is a button to open the form and complete the profile. A few days may pass between the first stage and the second.

To access the procedure: [determining professional level for tax regime](https://regimefiscale.unibocconi.it/index.php?lingua=ENG)

For more information: Administration and Finance Service, Collaborators Office 02-5836 2410 or 02-5836 2162 or e-mail [regimefiscale@uni-bocconi.it](mailto:regimefiscale@uni-bocconi.it)

### Collegamenti

- [Access the procedure](https://regimefiscale.unibocconi.it/index.php?lingua=ENG)

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus/services/determining-professional-level-tax-regimeold#addtoany "Show all")

text:
Campus

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus)


University mission and strategic objectives may require Faculty and Staff movements at a global level on work related activities such as conducting a research, attending a conference, meeting, students fairs and teaching.

These activities frequently involve travel abroad and be an enriching experience that can also involve exposure to certain risks.

Bocconi travel abroad policy establishes a set of guidelines and requirements for planning **international travel**, careful planning must precede international travel, even for seasoned travelers. Each travel destination requires a different strategy for responding to small inconveniences and serious emergencies.

Bocconi fundamental approach to managing the risks associated with travel is **to anticipate any possible risk** and take steps to prevent or at least reduce harm and protect travellers from exposure to undue risk. Key to this is **good preparation and prevention**. Pre-travel risk assement is therefore a crucial step of the travel itinerary plan.

Bocconi has established a Risk Rating System and contracted with a **travel assistance company** ( [ISOS - International SOS](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/campus-and-services/services/international-sos-faculty-and-satff)) to provide 24-hour worldwide travel security and medical assistance to its travelling employees.

text:
The application offers four types of services:
- **accessibility**: otherwise inaccessible documents – such as image files in GIF, TIFF, JPG, BMP, PCX, DCX, J2K, JP2, JPX, DJV formats and image-only PDFs, as well as all types of PDF files – can be converted into files with more accessible formats, including tagged PDF, DOC, DOCX, Word XML, XLS, XLSX, CVS, text, RTF and HTML.
- **audio**: documents can be converted into simple MP3 files _._
- **e-books**: documents can be converted into EPUB, EPUB3, EPUB3 with multimedia overlays and Mobi Pocket (Amazon Kindle) e-book formats. Also, the EPUB format can be converted to Mobi Pocket and vice versa.
- **Braille**: documents can be converted to and from Braille format. Documents can be formatted, outlined and delivered as print-ready files in a variety of digital Braille formats.
**Who Can Access It**

Sensus Access is an application specially designed for academic institutions. It is provided free of charge by Bocconi University to students, faculty and staff at the University and SDA for educational purposes and inclusive teaching.

**How It Works**
1. Upload the document (or a link to a web page) to SensusAccess
2. Select the format you want the document to be converted to
3. Enter your email address.
4. When your document is ready (after it has been converted), an email will be sent to your inbox.
5. Download the document from the email. The processed file will then be available for 7 days. After this deadline, the file will automatically be deleted from the server.
**Important:** the quality of the converted file depends on the quality of the original file. Therefore, only clearly legible documents should be uploaded.

**Terms of Use**
- The user of the service is solely responsible for the information contained in the files that are uploaded to SensusAccess. Therefore, documents containing personal data or confidential information should not be uploaded.

- The use of SensusAccess for processing copyrighted materials may constitute an infringement of copyright. The end user is solely responsible and shall indemnify the University from and against any claims regarding the improper use of this service by the end user.
**Information on the Data Processing Carried Out by the**

**SensusAccess Provider (Sensus Aps)**
- The use of SensusAccess does not require users to register for the service, which is integrated into the University's systems. SensusAccess does not collect personal information from users.
- The SensusAccess document conversion process is fully automated without any human intervention. Documents uploaded by the user are received on the SensusAccess server, processed and the result is returned to the user via email (which was entered at the bottom of the conversion form). The document uploaded by the user is subsequently deleted from the SensusAccess servers. Converted documents, available via encrypted links, are stored on SensusAccess servers for up to seven days and then deleted.
- SensusAccess does not use cookies to track, collect or store user data or preferences, but only to manage anonymous session data.
- All transaction logs are made anonymous and SensusAccess does not collect, store, transmit or otherwise manage personal information, or any other type of information sent by users to the service for conversion. Furthermore, SensusAccess does not share user information with third parties.
- The SensusAccess service is hosted on servers in the SensusAccess data center in Denmark. The facility is monitored and access is limited to authorized personnel. All servers are installed with state-of-the-art anti-malware protection and updated with the latest security level. Conversion requests sent by email may be detected, processed and stored in spam filters and other malware protection systems.

text:
## sPark of Change

sPark of Change is a project through which Bocconi University interprets the Olympic and Paralympic Games — and sports more broadly — as an economic, social and cultural phenomenon.

On the occasion of Milano Cortina 2026, the initiative offers an **outreach program open to the city**, grounded in data, research and analysis of the impact of sports on local territories, people and public policies.

The project highlights Bocconi’s longstanding and structured commitment to sports — in research, education and campus life — and presents it in an accessible form to a wider audience, with the aim of fostering understanding, dialogue and participation both within and beyond the campus.

[Discover the full program](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/news-and-events-hub/events/spark-change)

text:
Campus

Overview



Through its architecture and urban planning, the Bocconi campus expresses the University’s cultural vision. It features both innovation and avant-garde thinking, as well as an international focus that Bocconi shares with the city.

The University’s relationship with the city follows a unique model. The campus is fully integrated into the urban fabric and is just a 10-minute walk from bustling downtown Milan.

Each campus building is the work of important architects and is intended as an integral part of the city, prompting not only urban change, but also socio-economic evolution in the surrounding area.

Francesco Billari, Rector

I like to think of our campus as the "Fabbrica del Duomo", a typically Milanese idea: like Milan’s Duomo, it will never really be "completed", because such a marvel constantly needs small or big renovations allowing it to maintain its identity forever. This is exactly why, despite having recently completed construction on a gorgeous part of campus south of Via Sarfatti – composed of SDA Bocconi buildings, a residence hall, the Bocconi Sport Center and a public park – our campus is not yet done, and never will be.

text:
Housing

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing)

Life in one of the Bocconi Residence Halls is characterized by many advantages in terms of **security and opportunities to socialize**. However, it also requires the ability to adapt to certain limitations, including the impossibility to choose your flatmates and of guests overnight stay. Should you wish an accommodation alternative option, that may guarantee a greater degree of freedom, looking for an apartment might be a good solution.

metadata.heading_path: Bocconi Residence Halls | Bocconi University

text:
## **Lack of the requirements to use the Housing Service (merit and academic year enrollment)**

The housing assignment will be cancelled to those who at any time during the year are found to **lack one or more requirements to use the Housing service:** merit requirement or enrollment requirement for the 2024-25 academic year.

Such students will be required to vacate the accommodation immediately, **will still be required to pay in full any housing installments already due** and they will not be entitled to a refund if already paid.

They will not be entitled to a refund of the security deposit.

metadata.title: Cancellation | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **Lack of the requirements to use the Housing Service (merit and academic year enrollment)**

text:
## **Withdraw from studies or transfer to another university**

The housing assignment will be cancelled to those who will matriculate/enroll in A.Y. 2024-25 and who after matriculation/enrollment, at any time during the academic year, **transfer to another university or withdraw their studies**.

Such students will be required to vacate the accommodation immediately, **will still be required to pay in full any housing installments already due** and they will not be entitled to a refund if already paid.

They will not be entitled to a refund of the security deposit.

metadata.title: Cancellation | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **Withdraw from studies or transfer to another university**

text:
## **Failure to comply with payment deadlines**

The housing assignment will be cancelled to those who fail to pay the housing installment **after 15 days** from the due date.

Such students will be required to vacate the accommodation immediately, **will still be required to pay in full any housing installments already due** and they will not be entitled to a refund of installments already paid.

The student will not be entitled to a refund of the security deposit.

In this case, also the **suspension of the academic and administrative position of the student is applied** until he/she submits proof of completed payment regarding the amounts due.

The university also reserves the right in this case to order the ineligibility of the student to use the Housing Service for the entire duration of the study cycle.

metadata.title: Cancellation | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **Failure to comply with payment deadlines**

text:
## **Graduation**

The housing assignments will be cancelled for those who graduate during the year.

Such students will have to leave their assigned housing permanently by the end of the same month of graduation. They will be due (or not refunded) in this case the exit month and the following month.

The student will not be entitled to a refund of the security deposit.

metadata.title: Cancellation | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **Graduation**

text:
## **Missing economic requirement Right to university Education (for assignees at preferential rate)**

The housing assignment will be cancelled to those who are found to be assignees of preferential rate housing for a.y. 2024-25, but submit for the purpose of the Bocconi ISU Scholarship application a.y. 2024-25 an ISEE/ISEEU parificato whose values do not fall within the limits provided to benefit from preferential rate.

Such students will be required to vacate the accommodation immediately, **will still be required to pay in full any housing installments already due** and they will not be entitled to a refund if already paid.

They will not be entitled to a refund of the security deposit.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/cancellation#addtoany "Show all")

metadata.title: Cancellation | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **Missing economic requirement Right to university Education (for assignees at preferential rate)**

text:
Housing

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing)

In order to apply for one of the a.y. 2023-2024 Right to University Education benefits (ISU Bocconi Scholarship, preferential rate housing, preferential rate canteen service), students who meet all the requirements set **for each application** will also need to meet economic requirements defined at regional level. In order to attest their economic eligibility, applicants will be required to submit all the [necessary documentation](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/funding/2023-2024+necessary+documentation).

**Economic Requirements**

The ISEE/ISEEU parificato and ISPE/ISPEU parificato values are defined annually by the Lombardy Region with its own resolution.

For a.y. 2023-2024, the values set by the Lombardy Region will be the following:

>\> ISEE/ISEEU parificato no higher than € 24.335,11

>> ISPE/ISPEU parificato no higher than € 52.902,43

In order to apply for a benefit of the Right to University Education students will need to obtain an Attestazione ISEE per prestazioni agevolate per il diritto allo studio universitario issued in 2023 whose "ISEE" and "ISPE" values fall within the limits provided. Otherwise, the application cannot be accepted.

It is specified that the **ISPE** value is calculated by the University by dividing the ISP value by the equivalence scale.

Foreign students must obtain a substitute indicator for the ISEE and ISPE values called **ISEEU/ISPEU parificato**. This document can only be issued by the CAAF partnered with Bocconi University.

For all applicants, **citizens of non-EU Countries**, whose family household held assets at 31 December 2021 or produced income in 2021 in countries other than Italy, please note that, pursuant to Italian D.G.R. 2023 of Italian D.lgs 286/98, of Italian D.P.R. n. 394 of 31 August 1999, **declarations that attest an annual income for fiscal year 2021 of less than € 5,983.64 will not be considered valid** for the purposes of ISU Bocconi eligibility, as they are incompatible with immigration regulations.

**Economic requirements for INDEPENDENT STUDENTS**

metadata.title: Economic Requirements | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Economic Requirements | Bocconi University

text:
For the purpose of adequately accounting for individuals who bear the burden of the student's support, the student is considered independent only if, in addition to the economic requirements described above, **both** of the following requirements are met:

- **Residence**, as proven by registry data, outside the housing unit of the family of origin for at least two years before the date of the ISEEU parificato certificate, in an accommodation that is not owned or in usufruct by one of its members and therefore he/she uses an accommodation upon payment that must be documented (students are also not independent if at least one member holds the real right or personal right to use the accommodation);

- **Income** from employment or similar work, declared for tax purposes for at least the past two years, not less than €9,000.00annually. Work may not be performed under the employment of a family member.

If **both** the above conditions are NOT met – and duly documented – the student is considered part of the parents’ family unit, and therefore his/her economic and asset condition as well as that of his/her family of origin will be considered when determining eligibility for Right to University Education assistance and the ISU Bocconi bracket.

The additional documentation required to independent students is listed on the " **[Necessary documentation](https://www.unibocconi.it/wps/wcm/connect/bocconi/sitopubblico_en/navigation+tree/home/programs/current+students/services/funding/2023-2024+necessary+documentation)**" webpage.

**Attention**:

The family unit (Family household composition) is defined pursuant to the Decree of the President of the Council of Ministers 5 December 2013, n. 159.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/economic-requirements#addtoany "Show all")

metadata.title: Economic Requirements | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Economic Requirements | Bocconi University

text:
Housing

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing)


By accepting the housing assignment, the student commits to completing the following payments:

- the housing fee for the entire agreed-upon period;

- the housing deposit which will be returned only after the agreed-upon period.

The Housing fees in Bocconi University residences vary based on the type of residence and room and are divided in “full rate” and “preferential rate”.

The preferential rate of the accommodation service is applied to students who meet the requirements defined by the Right to University Education, based on the economic condition of the applicant's family evaluated on the basis of the value of the ISEE/ISSEU parificato indicator for services for the Right to University Education and the ISPE/ISPEU parificato value.

Students who meet the economic requirements (ISEE/ISEEU parificato and ISPE/ISPEU parificato parameters) foreseen for a.y. 2025-26 may request the assignment of a room at preferential rate. For the preferential rate, 3 different fee levels based on the ISU brackets (1st, 2nd and 3rd ISU bracket) are foreseen, depending on the values indicated on the ISEE/ISEEU Parificato Certificate submitted at the moment of the application.

For all other students the full rate is applied.

The students contemporary enrolled or who intend to contemporary enroll for a.y. 2025-26 also to another bachelor of science, master of science, integrated master of arts, AFAM (Institution of Higher Education for Fine Arts, Music and Dance) course of 1° or 2° degree of study, pursuant to Italian Law n. 33/2022, at a different Italian or foreign University or AFAM, who submit or have submitted the application for the benefit connected with the Right to University Education Assistance at the other University or AFAM, cannot benefit of the preferential rate housing assignment. These students will be able to submit the application only for full rate housing.

The students assigned with a preferential rate accommodation for whom the contemporary enrollment with the request of the benefit connected with the Right to University Education Assistance at the other University or AFAM is verified, will be requested to pay full rate. The new fee will be applied from the first installment.

The standard assignment period (late August 2025 — late June 2026) meets the academic needs of most students. The assignment for the month of July 2026 can be specifically requested and paid separately by those who need it. A special application to request the assignment for the month of July 2026 will be available, information to be published soon.

Housing rates may change according to the rate of inflation and any updates.

metadata.title: Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University

text:
### Housing fees a.y. 2025-26
- [Housing fees 2025-26.pdf](https://www.unibocconi.it/sites/default/files/Housing%20fees%202025-26.pdf)
The fees published here refer only to a.y. 2025-26 for which they applied. For subsequent academic years, housing fees may change according to the inflation rate and any updates.

#### **Deadlines for payment**

For a.y. 2025-26 the housing fees will be divided according to the following deadlines:
- **1st installment** (covering the period September — December 2025) **within 5 August 2025**

- **2nd installment** (covering the period January — March 2026) **within 19 December 2025**

- **3rd installment** (covering the period April — June 2026) **within 10 March 2026**

metadata.title: Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University > Housing fees a.y. 2025-26

text:
**ATTENTION**

It is specified that the possibility of seeing the MAV for the housing 1st installment will be open to all students that will result assignees of a room at the beginning of July, regardless of their situation in terms of reaching the merit and the enrollment requirement.

In fact, seeing the MAV payslip on you@B does not entail an unconditional room assignment, which is always subject to the possession of the enrollment and merit requirements, verified by the Office later (after the deadline for payment set for August 5th, 2025).

We remind you that, in case of lack of the requirements needed to use the accommodation, the housing assignment will be cancelled by the office and due payments will remain in charge to the student; the amounts due will not be cancelled.

It is also specified that:

The payment for the month of July 2026 will be added to the 3rd installment in case the student will request to extend his/her assignment period also for July 2026.

**Open reservations** for periods shorter than those foreseen for the annual assignments have a specific payment schedule: the deadlines will be communicated when the room is assigned.

Methods of payment

The payment of University fees can be made with PagoPa online system.

By clicking on the “ **Payment code**” and then selecting “ **PagoPA button**” you will be redirected to the online platform to complete the transaction by credit card, bank account or other payment methods (e.g. Paypal).

Choosing this payment method, the PagoPa online system will process the transaction within few minutes, with a significant reduction of registration times at Punto Blu and it will be confirmed by the color change from red to green.

Furthermore, as alternative payment method, After clicking on the “ **Payment code**”,  clicking on the “ **Print MAV**” bottom it will be possible to use the MAV printed online or the MAV code for the payment through [www.scrignopagofacile.it](http://www.scrignopagofacile.it/) or through your online bank in Italy.

It is specified that the time for the automatic registration of the payment, which will be confirmed by changing the color by red to green, in correspondence with the payment code, usually vary from 3 to 5 working days.

It is specified that **exclusively for extra sepa payments** (other than euros), it will also be possible to finalize the transaction through the **Flywire** payment tool.

This method will be selectable inside the financial situation – payments section within the Punto Blu, once you click on the specific payment code connected to the installment for which you intend to proceed.

It is specified that the automatic registration of the payment, through the change of color from red to green, will normally take place within **few minutes**.

In order not to incur penalties for late payment, it is advisable to finalize the transaction in advance of the expiry date.

If you need more details on how to make the payment, you can consult our guide available on **B in touch**.

The amount due for the housing 1st installment will be available for students with annual assignment and residual assignment received in June/July only after the enrollment to a.y. 2025-26.

Therefore, students enrolled in **years after the first** are advised to complete the **enrollment to the a.y. 2025-26 by July 31** in order to have enough time to pay the first housing installment before the check-in. Lastly, for students enrolled in the first year, the amount due will be available in yoU@B agenda starting from the last week of July.

Consequences when deadlines are not complied

If the payment is not registered in agenda within **15 days from the deadline** specified on the MAV:

metadata.title: Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University > Housing fees a.y. 2025-26

text:
• the **cancellation of the housing assignment is applied** with immediate expulsion from his/her assigned accommodation;

Also:

• the **suspension of the academic and administrative position** of the student is applied until he/she submits proof of completed payment regarding the amounts due.

The student will in any case and still be subject to the payment in full of all pending amounts and he/she will not have the right to the refund of amounts already paid. He/se will not have the right to the deposit refund.

The Fees, Funding and Housing Office, in addition, reserves the right to determine the non eligibility of the student for the Housing Service for the whole study cycle.

**ATTENTION**

A student who receives a disciplinary sanction of suspension from exams and partial exams during the year will not incur revocation of the housing assignment, however, if he or she has an assignment at preferential rate, he or she will undergo the **change of rate from preferential to full rate**, and will therefore be required to pay the housing fee at the full rate starting from the first installment due after the sanction is communicated.

With reference to PagoPa clicking on the “ **P** **ayment code**” and then selecting “ **PagoPA button**” you will be redirected to the online platform to complete the transaction by credit card, bank account or other payment methods (e.g. Paypal).

Choosing this payment method, the PagoPa online system will process the transaction within few minutes, with a significant reduction of registration times at Punto Blu and it will be confirmed by the color change from red to green.

MAV bank forms for housing fees will be available a few weeks before the payment deadline. In case of residual assignments, MAV bank forms will be available immediately after confirming the assignment.

Payments made through different methods will not be considered valid and will cause substantial delays and interruptions in the registration process and possible commissions withheld by the bank, which will be charged to the student.

The MAV slip for the payment of the housing 1st installment will be available for students with annual assignment and residual assignment received in June/July only after the enrollment to a.y. 2024-2025.

Therefore, students enrolled in **years after the first** are advised to complete the **enrollment to the a.y. 2024-2025 by July 31** in order to have enough time to pay the first housing installment before the check-in.

Lastly, for students enrolled in the first year the MAV slip will be available in yoU@B agenda starting from the last week of July.

After completion of payment, it is necessary to upload the receipt of payment in the check-in procedure.

metadata.title: Fees, Deadlines and Methods of payment | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: **Deadlines for payment**

text:
It is specified that **exclusively for extra sepa payments** (other than euros), it will also be possible to finalize the transaction through the **Convera** payment tool.

This method will be selectable inside the _financial situation – payments_ section within the Punto Blu, once you click on the specific payment code connected to the installment for which you intend to proceed.

It is specified that the automatic registration of the payment, through the change of color from red to green, will normally take place within 5 working days.

In order not to incur penalties for late payment, it is advisable to finalize the transaction in advance of the expiry date.

If you need more details on how to make the payment, you can consult our guide available on **B in touch**.

Consequences when deadlines are not complied

If the payment is not registered in agenda within **15 days from the deadline** specified on the MAV:

• the **cancellation of the housing assignment is applied** with immediate expulsion from his/her assigned accommodation;

Also:

• the **suspension of the academic and administrative position** of the student is applied until he/she submits proof of completed payment regarding the amounts due.

The student will in any case and still be subject to the payment in full of all pending amounts and he/she will not have the right to the refund of amounts already paid. He/se will not have the right to the deposit refund.

The Fees, Funding and Housing Office, in addition, reserves the right to determine the non eligibility of the student for the Housing Service for the whole study cycle.

**ATTENTION**

A student who receives a disciplinary sanction of suspension from exams and partial exams during the year will not incur revocation of the housing assignment, however, if he or she has an assignment at preferential rate, he or she will undergo the **change of rate from preferential to full rate**, and will therefore be required to pay the housing fee at the full rate starting from the first installment due after the sanction is communicated.

text:
A full rate assignment may be requested only by students who:
- **are non-resident**, meaning living in a municipality from which it is possible to reach the location of the study course attended by public transportation, with travel times exceeding 90 minutes(\*)
The only exception are students interested in applying during the Open reservation, for whom this requirement does not have to be strictly fulfilled.
- **Not fall under any of the** [**grounds for exclusion**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/grounds-exclusion-full-rate-housing "Grounds for exclusion - full rate housing")

- **having earned the minimum number of credits** as per paragraph 2 "Credits required to submit the application"

- **enrolled for the a.y. 2025-26:**

metadata.title: Full rate a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Full rate a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University

text:
>in the first, second, or third year of a Bachelor of Science program

>in the first, second, third, fourth, or fifth year of the Integrated Master of Arts in Law

>in the first, or second year of a Master of Science program.

**Students who are recipient of financial aid that includes** free **accommodation**(e.g., Una Scelta Possibile) or accommodation at reduced rate (e.g., Moving to Milan), upon acceptance of the benefit, renounce the possibility of receiving any another type of housing assignment (preferential rate, full rate) for the academic year of the first assignment and subsequent years (if the financial aid is renewed). Acceptance will result in the automatic cancellation of any housing request submitted and/or of any housing assignment received. It is also specified that after acceptance, no further housing requests of any type can be submitted.

The number of rooms available at full rate is usually lower than the applications submitted. Regularly enrolled students will be considered as a priority: applications submitted by students transferring from other Universities or repeating any year of course will not be accepted.

(\*) The travel times to be calculated are the minimum times required, based on the public transport route, between the departure station/stop from the place of residence and the arrival station/stop at the study location, including waiting times at intermediate stops. Walking routes or private transportation are excluded. These times can be verified on the regional website [www.muoversi.regione.lombardia.it](http://www.muoversi.regione.lombardia.it/) and, for cities outside the Lombardy Region, through the Google Maps search engine at [www.google.it/maps](http://www.google.it/maps)

2\. Credits required to submit the application

Exclusively for the full rate housing **renewal** application a minimum number of credits — detailed below — will be verified, which the student must have in order to apply. However, please note that no minimum number of credits is required:

It is specified that:

>First year students of the Bachelor or Integrated Master of Arts in Law programs are not required to reach this minimum number of credits if they wish to apply for the "Full rate new admission application (for 1st year students")

>A minimum number of credits is not required to students who wish to apply for the "Full rate new admission application (for all year students) application.

>A minimum number of credits is not required to students who wish to apply for the Open reservation application.

In the **"full rate housing renew application"** only applications from students who have achieved the number of credits specified below by the following deadlines will be considered: **February 1, 2025**, for bachelor, master of science, and **March 22, 2025**, for integrated master of arts in law programs.

**Minimum number of credits required**

**11 credits** for those enrolled in the first year (bachelor, master of science and integrated master of arts in law programs) in a.y. 2024-25

**60 credits** for those enrolled in the second year (bachelor and integrated master of arts in law programs) in a.y. 2024-25

**120 credits** for those enrolled in the third year of a bachelor programs in a.y. 2024-25and intending to enroll in the first year of a master of science program in Bocconi University for a.y. 2025-26, or for those enrolled in the third year of an integrated master of arts in law program in a.y. 2024-25

**180 credits** for those enrolled in the fourth year of an integrated master of arts in law program in a.y. 2024-25

metadata.title: Full rate a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Full rate a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University

text:
It is specified that for the achievement of the minimum number of credits required at the end of the winter session there is NO use of bonus credits in addition to the credits actually earned by the student.

For students who have participated in an Exchange program during the first semester, the timing of recognition of exams taken abroad will be considered. Such students are invited to still apply for housing renewal if interested and to monitor the credit recognition in order to complete it as soon as possible. Please note that no flexibility can be applied in relation to the achievement of the merit requirement for accommodation - credits to be achieved by August 10, 2025.

It is also specified that for students with disability pursuant to Italian Law n.104/1992 or with civil disability equal or greater than 66%, the number of minimum credits may deviate up to a maximum of 40% from the number of credits indicated above.

3\. Application submission

The housing assignment for a.y. 2025-26 is granted for the academic year of application only, therefore, it is not automatically renewed for subsequent academic years, but it is the student's responsibility to check whether it can be renewed and to make subsequent applications for assignment in the ways that will be provided and detailed on the website for each academic year.

Throughout the period in which the student is a resident in a Bocconi residence hall, room changes are not permitted. Instead, it is the right of Bocconi University to transfer the student, even during the year, to another residence hall at the rate provided for the new residence.

Before accessing the online application:

>please see the useful documents available at this [**link**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/useful-documents "Useful documents")

>keep your login credentials on hand (username and password)

>have a fast Internet connection and use a single device

**Full rate renewal application a.y. 2025-26**

**from 20 February to 5 March 2025**

Available only for students who are already guests at full rate as of 20 February 2025.

[**ONLINE APPLICATION**](https://admissions.unibocconi.it/admissions/Students/OpenFromExtUrl?concourseId=13198&ordType=)

**Full rate new admission application a.y. 2025-26**

Starting from March/April 2025

Rooms available in the Bocconi residence halls are limited. First year students (of Bachelor programs or of the Integrated Master of Arts in Law program) have a priority: in fact, the first application session is reserved to them. All other assignment sessions can be accessed by all students. In case of non-reservation due to the exhaustion of rooms in a specific session, it will still be possible to reapply in the following sessions. Please see the dedicated webpage with the Calendar of application sessions available for a.y. 2025-26 and carefully read the Instructions to submit the application.

[**Monthly Open reservations a.y. 2025-26**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/open-reservation-monthly-ay-2025-26 "Open reservation monthly a.y. 2025-26")

starting from September 2025

Please see the dedicated webpage with the **calendar of application sessions** available for a.y. 2025-26 and carefully read the Instructions to submit the application.

Applications submitted after the deadline and/or with submission methods that do not comply with the prescribed procedure and/or with incomplete documentation will not be taken into consideration.

After submitting your application, monitor in the "Notifications" area of MyApplication for communications from the Office, which may indicate the need for additions or integrations of documents.

How to upload the documents correctly

metadata.title: Full rate a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Full rate a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University

text:
You must print all documents, sign them by hand with a legible signature, and upload them with all pages to the designated section no later than the application’s deadline. In order to complete the application, it is necessary to click on the button "submit application" (inoltra domanda) after having completed the 600 euros housing deposit payment and the upload of all the documents required, that are available in the online application. The signature on the documents must be handwritten and match the signature on the ID submitted.

4\. Results and acceptance

**Result housing renewal application**

The result will be made available within early April 2025 on MyApplication. For the renewal application, no subsequent steps of acceptance are required, the room is assigned.

**Result housing new admission application and acceptance**

The information in the following paragraph is valid for all the new admission application sessions (1st year students and subsequent years students) and for the monthly open reservations.

The result of a housing new admission application is published on MyApplication usually around 3 working days after the session.

If the result is positive, you will receive a full rate housing assignment for a.y. 2024-25. On the result, you will be notified the residence assigned, the room type, the period of assignment and the fee applied. The accommodation offer received is not changeable and no room changes are foreseen.

If you wish to accept the offer of accommodation received, proceed to pay the security deposit of 500 euros and upload all the required documents by the date that will be indicated in the result. If you fail to complete all the steps required to accept the housing assignment received by that deadline, your assignment will automatically be cancelled.

5\. Requirements needed to use the accommodation

Students who will result housing assignees for a.y. 2025-26 following the procedure described above will be able to use the assigned accommodation only in presence of the following conditions:

**1) REGULAR ENROLLMENT for a.y. 2025-26**

\> regular enrollment for the absolute first time for a.y. 2025-26 in the first regular year of a Master of Science (i.e. bachelor Bocconi students going to the Master of Science program)

or

\> regular enrollment for a.y. 2025-26 for the absolute first time in a regular year subsequent to the first of a Bachelor of Science, Master of Science, Integrated Master of arts in Law or PhD program.

It is specified that the years of enrollment are counted for each study cycle starting from the absolute first year of enrollment\*.

It is specified that for students with a disability pursuant to Italian Law n.104/1992 or with civil disability equal or greater than 66% can reside in the Bocconi Residence Halls at a preferential rate also if enrolled for a.y. 2024-25 in the 2nd year “fuori corso” of a Bachelor of Science, Master of Science, Integrated Master of Arts in Law program.

\*The absolute first year of enrollment shall be understood as any academic year of first enrollment in any kind of degree program (even if different from the one attended in the a.y. 2025-26) which required the same education qualification as the one needed to access the attended program in the a.y. 2025-26, in any University in Italy or abroad, even if the program has not been completed.

**2) MERIT REQUIREMENT: CREDITS TO BE OBTAINED AND REGISTERED BY AUGUST 10, 2025**

It is specified that for the "New admission" and the "Monthly Open reservation" application sessions, the fulfillment of this requirements is not required.

metadata.title: Full rate a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Full rate a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University

text:
For the purpose of eligibility to use the accommodation, the rules established in terms of merit requirements and detailed on the [dedicated webpage](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/merit-requirements-ay-2025-26 "Merit requirements a.y. 2025-26") for the different types of housing applications apply.

6\. Payment

Before your arrival at the residence for a.y. 2025-26 you will have to pay the **housing 1st installment**: the deadline will be August 5th, 2025. You will see the amount due on your you@B once you'll have completed your enrollment for a.y. 2025-26. We recommend you to make the payment through the **Flywire tool** system.

Please consider that the calculation of the housing installment is carried out at the beginning of July for all housing assignees, regardless of their situation in terms of reaching the merit and the enrollment requirement (see point 5) that the office cannot verify before August 10. The possibility of seeing the due payment on you@B does **not** entail an unconditional room assignment, which is always subject to the possession of the enrollment and merit requirements. In absence of the requirements needed to use the assigned room, the office will proceed with the cancellation of your housing assignment, but you will still be required to pay the first installment (it will not be possible to cancel the due amount).

In the event you will pay the amount due but after a subsequent verification the merit and enrollment requirement will not be met, the amount you paid will not be refunded and the housing assignment will be cancelled in any case.

Please remember that the University grants the right to leave the accommodation early and to withdraw from the commitment to such payment, exclusively in the foreseen cases of renunciation of the accommodation, proven by the specific academic reasons required for the acceptance of the renunciation request, and withdrawal from the accommodation, according to what detailed on the dedicated website pages.

See all further details on the dedicated page [**Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2025-26**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/fees-deadlines-and-methods-payment-ay-2025-26 "Fees, deadlines and methods of payment a.y. 2025-26")

7\. Cancellation in case of lack of the requirements

Students assigned housing for whom it is subsequently determined that they did not regularly enroll for the a.y. 2025-26 or did not meet the merit requirements will lose their right (eligibility) to use the Housing Service for the a.y. 2025-26. The assignment will be cancelled by the office and students will be required to leave the assigned accommodation immediately. It is specified that the payments due will remain the responsibility of the student even in the event of cancellation of the assignment. For this reason, before submitting a housing application, students are invited to pay particular attention to the provisions regarding the [**cancellation**](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/applying-bocconi/bachelor-and-law-programs/housing/cancellation-ay-2025-26 "Cancellation a.y. 2025-26") of the housing assignment, specified on the dedicated webpage.

8\. Residence halls calendar and extraordinary openings

Bocconi residences will be open from late August 2025 to late June 2026. The standard assignment period for the 2025-26 academic year will meet the academic needs of most students.

There are no time restrictions for entry or exit for residence guests. However, there are restrictions for external guests, whose presence in the residence is allowed exclusively between 7:00 AM and 12:00 AM.

**Important check-out note**

metadata.title: Full rate a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Full rate a.y. 2025-26 | Bocconi University

text:
Regardless of the end date of your housing assignment, we remind you that at the end of your housing assignment period, you must completely clear your room and apartment of all your belongings, even if you will return to the same room the following year. In fact, as specified in the Bocconi Residences Regulations:

At the time of check-out for the end of the assignment period or during the year, the student is required to completely empty their room and other areas of their accommodation (bathroom, kitchen, living room, where present), including completely emptying refrigerators and freezers. Bocconi University is not responsible for goods left in the apartment/room after check-out.

Attention: rooms are reassigned to Summer School students during the summer, so it is not possible to leave anything inside your room or apartment. Any issues related to missing or deteriorated items during the summer, a period when the room is not assigned, cannot be considered by Bocconi University or the residence management.

Regarding stays during extraordinary opening periods (Christmas 2025 and July 2026), a separate request will be necessary (information available soon).

9\. Students with disabilities

Bocconi University has accommodations in each residence hall that are designed to accommodate students with disabilities in order to offer them the possibility to easily live their university experience in a collective context that meets their needs. Students with disabilities who require special housing needs may contact the Office of Fees, Funding and Housing, which will evaluate, with each student, which housing is best suited to their specific needs. All reported issues and provided documentation will be treated confidentially and subject to applicable privacy regulations. In order for the Office to be able to satisfy the requests received, it is best to clarify your needs in advance. Interested students are advised to contact the Fees, Funding and Housing Office before submitting their housing application.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/full-rate-ay-2025-26#addtoany "Show all")

text:
Housing

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing)


A security deposit of € 500.00 is required from students who accept the housing assignment to guarantee the fulfillment of all commitments made with the acceptance itself.

Payment of the security deposit must be made by credit card through StoreUB, following the instructions available within the housing application at the time of the reservation.

Please note that students who renew  the accommodation occupied will not have to repeat the payment of the previously paid security deposit.

The security deposit will be returned to the student, without interest, and thereafter at the time the student finally vacates their assigned place at the end of the academic year, but no later than January of the following year. This refund timeline will apply to all housing assignments related to the standard assignment period (end of August — end of June), including annual open reservations.

The deposit will be refunded under condition that the student has completed all the due housing payments and that he/she has no debts towards Bocconi University or the residence management. In addition, in case the student will not take in charge the possible restoration costs for damages made to the structures of the residence or for recovery of sanitary and general conditions of the room, the housing deposit will also be used for covering the related expenses.

When foreseen, the housing deposit refund will be made on a bank account in the student’s name that he/she will need to register on Punto Blu (Home > Personal data > Data Bank Account > Refunds).

The security deposit will NOT be refunded and will be kept as a penalty deposit in case of withdrawal from the housing assignment.

The security deposit will NOT be refunded in cases of housing renunciation not substantiated by the specific academic reasons provided for the acceptance of the request for withdrawal. More details on this page of the website. The security deposit will NOT, moreover, be returned in cases of revocation and cancellation of housing assignment.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/housing-deposit-ay-2024-25#addtoany "Show all")

text:
With this document, UNIVERSITA’ COMMERCIALE “LUIGI BOCCONI” (hereinafter Bocconi University or the University), with its registered office at Via Sarfatti 25, Milano, as Data Controller, hereby informs you, in compliance with European Regulation 2016/679 known as GDPR – General Data Protection Regulation, (hereinafter the Law), regarding use of the personal data (hereinafter the Data), collected when applying.
1. **The types of information we collect**
The personal data acquired are communicated by the student when registering or enrolling, such as full name, date and place of birth, gender, tax code, nationality, residence address, phone and electronic contact information, as well as data acquired with the application for benefit assignment, such as data related to vital data and the financial and asset situation of the student and the student’s family household. Some personal data are included in what is defined by the Law as belonging to “special categories” (hereinafter sensitive data), meaning information relating to health of the interested party and family members, information relating to ethnicity or race, etc.
2. **Purpose and modality of the Treatment**
The personal data described above are collected by Bocconi University through ISU Bocconi – the Student Assistance and Financial Aid Center/Fees, Funding and Housing Office and used, including with electronic instruments, for the sole purpose of allocating the benefit requested and verifying the conditions for allocation, in any case for legal purposes. Regarding the aforementioned purposes, the use of data will also take place with the use of electronic or automated means in a way that is relevant and limited to what is required for the purposes for which they are used and, in any case, in order to guarantee the strictest security and confidentiality and always in full compliance with current legislation. With regards to sensitive data, use will be carried out in proportion to the purpose pursued.
3. **The legal basis for processing your information**
The personal data described above shall be used following a request by the interested party and for legal purposes. They must, of necessity, be provided to determine the financial status of the applicant’s immediate and nuclear family and to determine whether they meet the requirements for accessing the benefit and determining the same, according to the criteria set out in the Housing Application Requirements and Regulations. Failure to consent to use of data shall prevent participation in the application.

For the purposes indicated at paragraph 2., the data can be treated without the consent of all interested parties, as necessary in compliance with a legal obligation pursuant to article 10 of Italian D.lgs. 68/2012, article 71 of Italian D.P.R. 445/2000 and article 11 of Italian DPCM 159/2013, as well as for the performance of the University tasks carried out in the public interest as an academic institution, in compliance with article 34, paragraphs 3 and 4 of the Constitution, DPCM 9 April 2001 and Italian D.Lgs. 68/2012.

Sensitive data communicated through the application for benefit assignment for the Right to University Education are treated in compliance with the aforementioned law (articles 6 and 9 of the General Data Protection Regulation n. 679/2016).
4. **Circulation and communication of Data**

metadata.title: Information on the Use of Personal Information and Rights of the Applicant (EU Regulations 2016/679) - Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: Information on the Use of Personal Information and Rights of the Applicant (EU Regulations 2016/679) - Bocconi University

text:
Data may be accessed by Staff of Bocconi University that have the need in order to perform duties or given their position held, as well as other entities providing services to Bocconi University, whom the University has specifically nominated as responsible or qualified for the processing of the Data of such subjects, inrelation to their duties, shall have access to the pertinent Data. All this, always in connectionwith the purposes pointed out previously. The data described above may be communicated to natural persons or legal entities that collaborate with the University for the purposes of allocating benefits for the Right to University Education; to public authorities such as the Lombardy Region, the Italian Ministry of Finance, the Italian Revenue Office and the Guardia di Finanza for legitimate checks. Data will be kept strictly for the time and scope for which they were collected.
5. **Data Subject’s Rights**
Data subjects have the following rights: to require the Data Controller to correct the personal data  held about them if it is incorrect; to require the Data Controller to erase their personal data; to require the Data Controller to restrict its Data processing activities (and, where its processing is based on the subject’s consent, the Data subject may withdraw that consent, without affecting the lawfulness of the Data Controller’s processing based on consent before its withdrawal); to object to the data treatment; to receive from the Data Controller the personal data  held about the subject which the subject has provided, in a reasonable format specified by the subject, including the purpose of the transmittal to another data controller. The aforementioned rights may be exercised with a request sent to ISU Bocconi – the Student Assistance and Financial Aid Center/Fees, Funding and Housing Office at the email address [funding@unibocconi.it](mailto:funding@unibocconi.it) to the attention of the internal Data Processor.

Please note that the above rights are not absolute, and we may be entitled to refuse requests where exceptions apply.

The Data Controller for personal data is Università Commerciale Luigi Bocconi.

The internal Data Processor of the data provided is Mr. Paolo Cancelli, Director of the Students Outreach and Support Organizational Unit.

The Data Controller has appointed the Data Protection Officer ("DPO") that may be contacted at the email address [dpo@unibocconi.it](mailto:dpo@unibocconi.it) for any questions regarding use of personal data and to exercise the rights set out in the Law.

text:
Housing

[Overview](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing)

Accommodation availability and Summer School opportunity

Standard accommodation assignments end for everyone on **27 of June, 2026**.

It will not be possible to request an extension in your current assignment for the month of July, but any student who has already been assigned accommodation in one of the Bocconi residences in June 2026 may still request a room in the Isonzo residence, subject to availability. Please read the paragraphs below carefully with regard to check-out in June and the options available for July.

Check out June 2026

Regardless of the end date of your housing assignment, we remind you that **at the end of your housing assignment period, you must completely clear your room and apartment of all your belonging** s, even if you will return to the same room the following year. In fact, as specified in the Bocconi Residences Regulations:

_At the time of check-out for the end of the assignment period or during the year, the student is required to completely empty their room and other areas of their accommodation (bathroom, kitchen, living room, where present), including completely emptying refrigerators and freezers. Bocconi University is not responsible for goods left in the apartment/room after check-out._

Attention: rooms are reassigned to Summer School students during the summer, so it is not possible to leave anything inside your room or apartment. **Any issues related to missing or deteriorated items during the summer, a period when the room is not assigned, cannot be considered by Bocconi University or the residence management.**

In case of need of storage, we suggest you to evaluate very common luggage storage services in Milan, such as BoxOK, Red Box Storage, Radical storage, Casaforte Milano

Staying in Milan: book your accommodation

Students already staying in the residence halls during the a.y. 2025-26 (during the month of June of 2026) who need to stay in Milan in July as well, the University has reserved an entire building, **Isonzo Residence**, which with about 200 total available places will be able to **accommodate all students in need of staying in Milan in July** (assignment period 27 of June - 27 of July, 2026). In August, however, all Bocconi residence halls will be closed.

It will be possible to check the availability in Isonzo Residence and **book a room for the month of July** through a dedicated application, available from February 3 until all available places are filled, and in any case no later than May 18, 2026. All those who book will be required to pay the July monthly rate based on the same rate applied (full or preferential) depending on their annual assignment a.y. 2025-26.

Rooms in Isonzo will be available:

>Full rate: 750,00 euros

>Preferential rate: 375,00 euros

Students already staying in Isonzo will be considered the same as those staying in other residences for the purpose of assigning accommodation in the Isonzo residence for the month of July; in case of booking, any room in the residence may be assigned to them.

Reservations confirmed for the month of July cannot be subsequently cancelled; the expected amount will still be due from the student even if their need to stay in Milan for the month of July no longer applies.

Rooms will be assigned following the application order and until all available places are filled. In the event that the number of requests exceeds the availabilities, the office will try to find an alternative solution.

**Attention: this application IS NOT AVAILABLE FOR SUMMER SCHOOL STUDENTS**, who have their dedicated options.

metadata.title: July 2026 | Bocconi University
metadata.heading_path: July 2026 | Bocconi University

text:
This opportunity is aimed at and reserved for students:

- adults (at least 18 years old)
- already guests in a residence hall as of the end date of the standard assignment period (June 27, 2026)
- interested in staying in Milan during the month of July 2026
- enthusiastic about being able to actively contribute to the life of our community by serving during the Summer School period.

**Selected students will be able to stay in their original residence until the 27 of July 2026 free of charge and will receive a cash compensation to cover small expenses of 500.00 euros.**

**Role and commitment**

Summer School Residence Ambassadors will facilitate summer school students upon arrival and their stay in the residence by quickly sharing practical and useful information, helping them solve minor daily problems, and properly directing them to the internal contact personnel in case of need. They will not, in fact, have specific responsibility for guests’ health or safety issues, but they will still be informed of the protocols in place and will be able to refer students to support staff in a timely and correct manner.

They will also support the residence hall manager in all small additional administrative activities related to the Summer School students' stay during the period. Finally, they will be in touch with the relevant Bocconi offices for all information or needs related to the Summer School students staying in the residence.

Finally, they may be contacted or involved by offices to channel specific organizational information through them regarding one or more resident students. In the same way, they may approach the residence staff and offices to report special issues or situations they may have become aware of.

Their availability is required at various times throughout the entire period from **5 of July (morning) to 18 of July (afternoon).**

Summer School Residence Ambassadors will have to:

- Be present and on call at the residence for the duration of their assigned shifts
- Welcome summer school students and their families during check-in
- Provide initial guidance information upon arrival (route to/from the University, public transportation, supermarkets, pharmacies...)
- Convey student requests through appropriate channels (e.g., maintenance app, email, phone call depending on the situation)
- Indicate to summer school students how to act/who to contact in case of need
- Helping the residence manager to share important information about the residence, room and apartment amenities, contact personnel
- Support the residence manager in communicating with students and offices (filling out files, various verifications)
- Verify student room attendance, if requested by offices
- Report any necessary information about the residence hall Summer school guests, if requested by the offices
- Accompany the residence manager or security personnel to the apartments if needed
- support check-out operations until activities are fully completed

**Apply!**

To apply, you will need to send an email to [housing@unibocconi.it](mailto:housing@unibocconi.it) from 23 of February to 8 of March, 2026

For each residence 3 to 4 ambassadors will be selected.

Applications will be evaluated by the Office in order of arrival, but also taking into account the candidate's aptitude as expressed in a short letter of motivation; any previous experience in line with the required activity; and a fair gender distribution with respect to the total number of students appointed.

The selected students will then be contacted by the office indicatively around mid-April. They will be asked to sign an assumption of office document, provide bank details useful for payment of the compensation and participate in two half-day training sessions with the offices in June, prior to the start of the program.

[More…](https://www.unibocconi.it/en/current-students/housing/july-2026#addtoany "Show all")
